items.title: fix: harden responses API probing and fallback selection

## Summary
- harden onboarding so NVIDIA Endpoints, Gemini, and other OpenAI-compatible endpoints only favor /responses when a tool-calling probe matches OpenClaw behavior
- fall back to /chat/completions when /responses returns 200 but does not produce compatible tool-call output
- document the updated selection policy and add regression coverage for the fallback paths

## Testing
- npx vitest run src/lib/http-probe.test.ts test/onboard.test.js test/onboard-selection.test.js

items.title: Downgrade transient plugin refresh ECONNRESET/timeout failures to concise warn/debug logs
items.content:
## Summary
The Jeeves OpenClaw plugin refresh helper currently logs full scary stack traces when background content cache refreshes hit transient local HTTP failures such as ECONNRESET or request timeouts.

In practice these appear to be recoverable, low-risk failures in async menu/content refresh, not service outages. The current logging creates noise and makes healthy systems look broken.

## Problem
Current helper behavior:
- createAsyncContentCache() catches refresh failures via onError
- default handler does console.warn('[jeeves] async content cache refresh failed:', err)
- transient Undici fetch errors therefore emit full stack traces
- prior cached content is retained anyway, so the visible impact is usually nil

Observed examples:
- TypeError: fetch failed with cause Error: read ECONNRESET
- request timeout / abort during background refresh

Affected call sites observed:
- jeeves-watcher OpenClaw plugin background menu refresh
- jeeves-runner OpenClaw plugin background content refresh

## Desired behavior
Patch the shared Jeeves plugin refresh helper so that transient network-ish failures are downgraded to concise single-line warn/debug messages instead of full stack traces.

Example shape:
- [jeeves] async content cache refresh transient failure: ECONNRESET reading http://127.0.0.1:1936/status
- [jeeves] async content cache refresh timed out after 10000ms for http://127.0.0.1:1937/status

Keep stack traces for genuinely unexpected/non-transient failures.

## Proposed approach
- Classify transient fetch failures by error/cause:
  - ECONNRESET
  - timeout / AbortError
  - possibly ETIMEDOUT, UND_ERR_CONNECT_TIMEOUT, similar socket-level transient cases
- Log those as concise warn/debug lines with endpoint context
- Preserve previous cached content exactly as now
- Optional: add lightweight retry/backoff so we do not log every refresh miss during short flaps
  - e.g. one immediate retry or next-attempt cooldown/backoff window
- Consider exposing a shared help

items.title: [💡 想法] 环境感知型 Hook Context Injection Framework
items.content:
## 背景

superpowers 的 session-start hook 支持多平台检测：
- 检测 `COPILOT_CLI` 环境变量 → 触发 Copilot CLI 特定上下文注入
- 检测 `CURSOR_PLUGIN_ROOT` / `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` → 触发 Cursor 特定行为
- 检测 `CLAUDE_CODE` → Claude Code 标准行为

这种 **环境感知型 context injection** 使得同一套 hooks 可以服务多个平台。

## 目标

在 OpenClaw 中实现类似的环境感知型 hook 框架，支持多运行时自动适配。

## 子任务

- [ ] 分析 OpenClaw 当前 hooks 机制
- [ ] 设计 `session-start` hook 的环境检测策略（平台 + runtime 检测）
- [ ] 实现 context injection 的条件触发（不是每次都全量注入）
- [ ] 支持 hooks 链式组合：多个 hook 按优先级顺序执行
- [ ] 添加测试覆盖：模拟不同环境变量的注入结果
- [ ] 整理 hooks API 文档

## 参考

- superpowers: `hooks/session-start`
- superpowers v5.0.7: Copilot CLI sessionStart context injection
- superpowers v5.0.3: Cursor hooks (camelCase format)

items.title: fix(qqbot): honor audioAsVoice for plain reply TTS
items.content:
## Summary

- **Problem:** QQ Bot ignored `audioAsVoice` on normal (non-`QQBOT_PAYLOAD`) agent replies, so TTS never ran and `onMessageSent` saw `mediaType`/`ttsText` undefined even when the flag reached `deliver`.
- **Fix:** Run the same TTS + voice send path as structured audio payloads when `audioAsVoice` is set; pass gateway `cfg` into plain-reply delivery so TTS settings resolve. Extract `synthesizeAndDeliverTtsVoice` for reuse.
- **Scope:** QQ Bot plugin only (`extensions/qqbot`); changelog entry under Unreleased.

Fixes #62831

## Tests

```
pnpm test -- extensions/qqbot/src/outbound-deliver.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts
```

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Integrations (QQ Bot channel)

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No** (same TTS paths as structured payload)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **No**

## Compatibility

- Backward compatible: **Yes**
- Config/env changes: **No** (existing TTS config semantics unchanged)


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

items.title: [💡 想法] System Prompt → User Message Injection：减少 Token 重复开销
items.content:
## 背景

obra/superpowers v5.0.7 做了关键修复：将 bootstrap 从 `experimental.chat.system.transform` 移到 `experimental.chat.messages.transform`，以 **prepending to first user message** 替代 system message。

**收益：**
- 避免 system message 在每轮重复（token bloat）
- 修复 Qwen 等模型在多个 system message 下失效的问题
- 减少对话历史中的 token 累积

## 目标

评估 OpenClaw 的 system prompt 注入策略，引入 user message prepend 模式降低 token 开销。

## 子任务

- [ ] 分析 OpenClaw 当前 system prompt 注入时机和方式
- [ ] 研究将 bootstrap/context 注入到 first user message 的可行性
- [ ] 对比两种注入方式的 token 消耗差异
- [ ] 评估对 model compatibility（Qwen 等多 system message 不友好模型）的影响
- [ ] 设计 fallback 策略（某些 model 仍需 system message）
- [ ] 实现并测试

## 参考

- superpowers PR #910: bootstrap as user message instead of system message
- Issue: #750 (token bloat), #894 (Qwen compatibility)
- https://github.com/obra/superpowers/releases/tag/v5.0.7
items.author: ThomasLiu

items.title: [💡 想法] Inline Self-Review Checklist: 30秒自检替代25分钟子Agent评审
items.content:
## 背景

obra/superpowers v5.0.6 做了一个关键决策：用 **inline self-review checklist** 替换 **subagent review loop**。
- subagent review loop: ~25分钟开销，质量无显著提升
- inline self-review: ~30秒，等效缺陷率
- 测试数据：5个版本 × 5次trial，质量分无差异

## 目标

将 self-review checklist 模式引入 OpenClaw agent，降低评审开销。

## 子任务

- [ ] 研究 superpowers 的 spec self-review 和 plan self-review checklist 内容
- [ ] 设计适合 OpenClaw 的 inline self-review prompt 模板
- [ ] 实现 skill/agent 自检流程（在关键节点注入 checklist）
- [ ] 测试对比：token消耗、执行时间、缺陷率
- [ ] 整理最佳实践文档

## 参考

- superpowers: `skills/brainstorming`, `skills/writing-plans`
- v5.0.6 Release Notes: https://github.com/obra/superpowers/releases/tag/v5.0.6

items.title: Bug: [v2026.4.5] memory-core 'Dreaming' Feature Fails to Initialize Silently Despite Exhaustive Troubleshooting
items.content:
### Summary

The `Dreaming` memory consolidation feature within the stock `memory-core` plugin on **version 2026.4.5** fails to initialize under any circumstances on our production Linux server. The failure is completely silent: no errors are logged (even at trace level), and none of the expected artifacts (e.g., `DREAMS.md`, `.dreams/` directory, cron job registration) are created.

This bug was investigated exhaustively over several hours. We have ruled out configuration errors, plugin conflicts, and dependency issues, pointing strongly to an internal bug in the plugin's initialization logic for this version.

### Environment

- **OpenClaw Version:** 2026.4.5 (3e72c03)
- **OS:** OpenCloudOS (Linux 6.6.117-45.1.oc9.x86_64)
- **Node Version:** v24.14.1
- **Deployment:** Headless server, managed via SSH.

### Expected Behavior

After correctly configuring `openclaw.json` and restarting the gateway, the Dreaming system should:
1. Create a `DREAMS.md` file in the workspace.
2. Create a `.dreams/` directory in the memory path.
3. Register a cron job named 'Memory Dreaming Promotion' in `cron/jobs.json`.
4. Log its initialization, or at least throw an error if it fails.

### Actual Behavior

**Complete silence.** None of the expected files or directories are created. The cron job is not registered. The gateway starts without any errors related to `memory-core` or `dreaming`.

### Detailed Troubleshooting Steps Performed

We followed a methodical, multi-hour process to debug this, eliminating every possible external factor:

**1. Initial Configuration (By the book)**
- Set `plugins.entries["memory-core"].config.dreaming.enabled = true`.
- **Result:** Failed. No files created.

**2. Enabled Global Cron Service**
- Found that `dreaming` depends on the global cron service.
- Set `cron.enabled = true` in `openclaw.json`.
- **Result:** Still failed.

**3. Isolated Conflicting Plugins**
- Identified other memory-related plugins: `openclaw-mem0` and `memory-lancedb-pro`.
- Disab

items.title: 📅 AI Agent & Skills 每日情报简报 - 2026-04-08
items.content:
**📅 AI Agent & Skills 每日情报简报 (2026-04-08)**

**🚀 今日头条**
1. **Anthropic 发布 Project Glasswing**：旨在保障 AI 时代的关键软件安全，为 Agent 部署提供新的安全基准。
2. **Z.ai 推出 GLM-5.1 模型**：重点优化长 horizon 任务处理能力，显著提升 Agent 在复杂流程中的表现。
3. **OpenClaw 更新 v2026.4.7**：新增 `openclaw infer` 一级命令，强化跨模型、媒体及 Web 的推理工作流支持。
4. **Anthropic 发布 Claude Mythos Preview System Card**：披露最新模型系统卡细节，为开发者提供透明度参考。
5. **主流 Agent 框架密集更新**：LangGraph 与 crewAI 同日发布新版本，聚焦 CLI 体验与追踪治理。
6. **AI 安全与长任务成为焦点**：今日动态显示行业正从单一模型能力向安全部署与长程任务解决转移。

**📦 GitHub 亮点项目**
- **🦀 OpenClaw (v2026.4.7)**：本次更新核心在于 CLI 增强，新增 `openclaw infer` 枢纽，支持 provider-backed 推理工作流，涵盖模型、媒体、Web 及 Embedding 任务，大幅简化技能调用流程。
- **🦜 LangGraph (cli==0.4.21)**：新增 `validate` 命令，强化工作流验证能力，提升 Agent 构建的稳定性。
- **🤖 crewAI (1.14.0)**：新增 checkpoint list/info CLI 命令，引入 `guardrail_type` 区分追踪，并添加 SqliteProvider 支持，增强本地化与可观测性。

**📚 研究进展**
- **GLM-5.1: Towards Long-Horizon Tasks**：Z.ai 最新研究表明，通过架构优化显著提升模型在长周期任务中的规划与执行一致性，解决 Agent 中途迷失痛点。
- **Claude Mythos Preview System Card**：Anthropic 公开预览版系统卡，详细说明了模型能力边界与安全对齐措施，为高风险场景应用提供评估依据。

**🛠️ 技术动态**
- **OpenClaw 技能协议更新**：新版本通过 `infer` 命令标准化了推理工作流，虽未直接提及 MCP，但其 provider-backed 架构为兼容 MCP 等工具协议奠定了底层基础，强化了 Agent Skills 的互操作性。
- **Agent 开发体验优化**：LangGraph 与 crewAI 的 CLI 更新表明，开发者工具链正趋向成熟，验证（Validate）与守卫（Guardrail）成为标配功能。
- **软件安全新标准**：Project Glasswing 的推出预示着 AI 原生软件的安全审计将成为 Agent 上线前的必要环节。

**🇨🇳 中文社区**
- **GLM-5.1 引发关注**：智谱 AI（Z.ai）发布的 GLM-5.1 针对长 horizon 任务的优化，预计将成为今日知乎、掘金及微信公众号的技术讨论热点，建议开发者关注其在复杂 Agent 流程中的实际表现。
- **公众号特别提示**：基于今日数据源，暂无特定公众号文章收录，但建议密切关注各大 AI 技术号对 GLM-5.1 及 OpenClaw 新特性的深度解读。

**📺 视频动态**
- **暂无重点更新**：今日数据源中未包含 B 站或 YouTube 相关新教程或视频发布。

**🌟 趋势观察**
今日核心趋势显示 AI Agent 发展已进入“深水区”，重点从模型能力转向**长程任务可靠性**、**工具链标准化（OpenClaw/CLI）**及**部署安全性**。

**🔗 参考链接**
1. Project Glasswing: https://www.anthropic.com/glasswing
2. GLM-5.1 Blog: https://z.ai/blog/glm-5.1
3. Claude Mythos System Card: https://www-cdn.anthropic.com/53566bf5440a10affd749724787c8913a2ae0841.pdf
4. OpenClaw Release v2026.4.7: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4

items.title: Multi-repo targeting, real git identity, and persistent agent memory
items.content:
The workflow was hardcoded to operate on its own repo, committed as `openclaw[bot]`, and had no cross-run memory since Actions runners are ephemeral.

### Repository targeting
- New `ALLOWED_REPOS` Actions variable — comma-separated `owner/repo` list
- New `repositories` workflow_dispatch input for manual override
- Random selection from the list each run; single repo works unchanged
- Falls back to `github.repository` when unconfigured

```
# Settings → Variables → Actions
ALLOWED_REPOS = myuser/project-a, myuser/project-b, myuser/side-project
```

### Real git identity
- Resolves PAT owner's name/email via `gh api user` at the start of each run
- Falls back to `<id>+<login>@users.noreply.github.com` for private emails
- Removed all `openclaw[bot]` references — commits, safety-net messages, skill instructions

### Agent memory
- Agent reads/writes `.openclaw/memory.md` in each target repo
- Prompt dynamically adapts based on whether the file exists
- Skill instructs the agent to keep it under ~80 lines, summarizing older entries
- No external storage needed — memory travels with the repo

### Skill updates
- Added memory recall/update steps to the workflow
- Added explicit rules against revealing AI/bot authorship in commits or comments
- Skills loaded via `skills.load.extraDirs` so they work when operating on external repos

items.author: dependabot[bot]

items.title: [提效想法] Worktree Manager Skill - 任务级 Git 隔离
items.content:
## 背景

ecc2 每个 Session 都有独立的 Git Worktree（ecc/src/worktree/mod.rs），实现：
- 每个子任务在独立分支工作，不污染主分支
- Worktree 之间完全隔离，互不干扰
- 支持快速清理和创建

这对 AI Coding 场景非常有价值：大型重构可以并行尝试多个方案而不冲突。

## 目标

实现 worktree-manager Skill：
1. **创建 Worktree**: 基于任务 ID 创建独立 Git Worktree
2. **切换 Worktree**: 任务切换时自动保存和切换 Worktree
3. **清理 Worktree**: 任务完成后自动清理 Worktree
4. **Diff 预览**: 显示各 Worktree 相对于 base 的变更统计

## 子任务

- 实现 git worktree create/remove/list 操作封装
- 实现 Worktree 生命周期与 Session 绑定
- 实现 worktree diff-summary 统计
- 与 OpenClaw sessions_spawn 集成

## 参考

ecc2/src/worktree/mod.rs 的 create_for_session 和 remove 函数
items.author: ThomasLiu

items.title: [提效想法] Inbox Task Queue Skill - Agent 间异步任务传递
items.content:
## 背景

ecc2 的 Session 间通信通过 Inbox 机制实现（基于文件系统），提供了：
- 任务委托（Task Handoff）：Lead -> Delegate 传递任务
- 消息持久化：即使 Agent 重启消息不丢失
- 已读/未读状态追踪

OpenClaw 当前 sessions_spawn 是无状态的，不支持子 Agent 之间的异步通信和任务传递。

## 目标

实现 inbox-task-queue Skill（基于文件系统或 SQLite）：
1. **TaskQueue**: 任务入队，按优先级排序
2. **Agent Inbox**: 每个子 Agent 有独立 Inbox
3. **Handoff**: 支持 Lead -> Delegate 任务移交
4. **状态追踪**: 任务状态（pending/running/completed/failed）

## 子任务

- 设计 Inbox 存储格式（JSON 文件或 SQLite）
- 实现 enqueue/dequeue 逻辑
- 实现 Inbox 路由（根据 agent 标签路由到对应 Inbox）
- 实现任务状态持久化和查询
- 与 team-orchestration Skill 集成

## 参考

ecc2/src/session/store.rs 的消息存储和 inbox 路由逻辑
items.author: ThomasLiu

items.title: [提效想法] Team Orchestration Skill - 多 Agent 协作编排
items.content:
## 背景

ecc2 系统实现了基于 Session 的任务委托机制：Lead Agent 可以将任务分发给 Delegate Agent，每个 Delegate 在独立 Git Worktree 中执行，结果汇总回 Lead。这解决了复杂任务分解并行执行的难题。

当前 OpenClaw 的 sessions_spawn 可以创建子 Agent，但缺乏：
- 任务队列和分发机制
- 子 Agent 结果汇总
- 团队状态监控

## 目标

实现 team-orchestration Skill，支持：
1. **任务分解**: 将复杂任务自动拆分为子任务
2. **并行分发**: 基于 sessions_spawn 并行执行子任务
3. **结果汇总**: 收集所有子 Agent 结果，合并输出
4. **超时处理**: 子 Agent 超时自动取消并重试

## 子任务

- 设计任务分解策略（按文件/按模块/按步骤）
- 实现 useTeamOrchestration composable
- 实现结果树形合并逻辑
- 实现超时和错误处理
- 编写使用示例和测试用例

## 参考

ecc2 session/manager.rs 的 assign_session 和 drain_inbox 逻辑
items.author: ThomasLiu

items.title: Group chat session cold-start triggers redundant botcord_room_context calls
items.content:
## Summary

New group chat sessions repeatedly call `botcord_room_context` (room_summary / room_messages) on every turn because the LLM has no historical context for the current room at session start.

## Observed Behavior

In the `rm_de5f86528dfb` (botcord 内部试用群) group session, `botcord_room_context` is called starting from the 2nd LLM turn (`msgs=3`) and continues on nearly every subsequent turn. The session never underwent compaction — it grew from 1 to 23 messages organically.

| LLM Turn | msgs in context | cumulative room_context calls |
|----------|----------------|-------------------------------|
| 1        | 1              | 0                             |
| 2        | 3              | 1                             |
| 3        | 5              | 1                             |
| 4        | 7              | 2                             |
| 5        | 9              | 2                             |
| 6        | 11             | 3                             |
| 7        | 13             | 3                             |
| 8        | 15             | 3                             |
| 9        | 17             | 3                             |
| 10       | 19             | 3                             |
| 11       | 21             | 3                             |
| 12       | 23             | 4                             |

In contrast, the long-running `rm_8863360fe196` session (76-94 msgs, rich history) had **zero** `room_context` calls across 13 LLM invocations.

## Root Cause Analysis

The two context injection layers in `plugin/src/room-context.ts` do not provide current-room conversation history:

| Layer | What it injects | What's missing |
|-------|----------------|----------------|
| **Static** (`appendSystemContext`) | Room name, description, rule, members | No recent messages |
| **Dynamic** (`prependContext`) | Cross-room activity digest (OTHER rooms' last 3 msgs) | No CURRENT room messages |

When a new group session starts, the LLM sees:
1.

items.title: Bug: memory-core 'Dreaming' feature silently fails to initialize in v2026.4.5
items.content:
### Describe the bug

The new `Dreaming` memory consolidation feature (part of the `memory-core` plugin) fails to initialize or create any of its required files/directories/cron jobs on version 2026.4.5, despite being configured exactly as documented. The failure is completely silent; there are no errors in standard logs, and even under `--log-level trace`, it simply appears to ignore the configuration.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Start with a clean or existing `openclaw.json` configuration.
2. Enable global cron:
   ```json
   "cron": {
     "enabled": true
   }
   ```
3. Configure and enable `memory-core` and `dreaming` according to the docs:
   ```json
   "entries": {
     "memory-core": {
       "enabled": true,
       "config": {
         "dreaming": {
           "enabled": true,
           "frequency": "0 3 * * *"
         }
       }
     }
   }
   ```
4. Ensure `memory-core` is in the `plugins.allow` array.
5. Ensure no conflicting memory plugins (like `openclaw-mem0` or `memory-lancedb-pro`) are enabled or present in the `extensions` directory.
6. Start or restart the gateway: `openclaw gateway`

### Expected behavior

1. The gateway should start normally.
2. A `DREAMS.md` file should be created in the workspace.
3. A `.dreams/` directory should be created under the memory directory.
4. A cron job for dreaming should be visible in the internal scheduler (e.g., in `cron/jobs.json`).

### Actual behavior

None of the expected files or directories are created. The internal cron scheduler (`cron/jobs.json`) does not register a job for dreaming. There are absolutely no errors thrown in the standard gateway logs. Running `openclaw doctor` reports no issues with the `memory-core` plugin.

### Environment

- **OS:** OpenCloudOS (Linux 6.6.117-45.1.oc9.x86_64)
- **Node Version:** v24.14.1
- **OpenClaw Version:** 2026.4.5 (3e72c03)
- **Gateway Mode:** local

### Additional context

- I have meticulously verified the JSON syntax.
- I initially included `"timezone": "As

items.title: [2026-04-07] Are We Idiocracy Yet?
items.content:
# Hacker News
Are We Idiocracy Yet? ## 智力倒退：追踪《白头神探》中现实与设想的距离 (Tracking How Close Reality Is to Mike Judge's Idiocracy) 《白头神探》(Idiocracy) 是一部 2006 年的科幻喜剧电影，由 Mike Judge 执导，讲述了一个智力低下的人工建造者 Joe Dirt 在未来醒来后，发现自己是地球上最聪明的人的故事。电影以讽刺的方式描绘了一个由于大众智力水平持续下降而导致社会倒退的未来世界。本文旨在追踪现实世界与电影中描绘的未来之间的距离。 **电影中的主要设定与预言:** * **智力水平下降:** 电影的核心是人类智力水平逐渐下降的趋势，导致社会功能失灵，文化退化，科技停滞。 * **媒体的低俗化:** 电影中的媒体充斥着低俗、简单的娱乐节目，缺乏深度和信息量。 * **过度依赖科技:** 电影展现了一个过度依赖自动化和低劣科技的社会，这些科技最终因缺乏维护和理解而崩溃。 * **环境恶化:** 电影描绘了一个环境污染严重，资源枯竭的世界。 * **政治腐败:** 政治体系被简化和腐败，决策缺乏远见和理性。 * **大众消费主义:** 社会被过度消费主义所驱动，人们追求肤浅的物质享受。 * **饮食文化:** 电影中食物以高度加工、营养不良的食品为主，反映了对健康饮食的忽视。 **现实世界的对比与进展:** 虽然现实世界尚未完全达到《白头神探》描绘的极端情况，但电影中提出的许多担忧在一定程度上正在发生。 * **智力水平：** 关于全球平均智商下降的争论一直存在。一些研究表明，发达国家的智商水平在过去几十年有所下降，这可能与教育质量下降、营养不良以及过度使用科技娱乐有关。然而，其他研究则表明，智商水平仍然在缓慢上升，这可能与更好的营养和教育有关。 * **媒体环境:** 电影中媒体的低俗化在现实世界中也有所体现。社交媒体和短视频平台上的内容往往趋向于简单、快速和娱乐化，这可能导致人们对复杂问题的关注度下降。 * **科技发展与依赖:** 科技的快速发展无疑提高了生产力和便利性，但同时也带来了对科技的过度依赖。自动化和人工智能的普及也引发了对就业和技能需求的担忧。 * **环境问题:** 环境污染、气候变化和资源枯竭是现实世界面临的严峻挑战，与电影中对环境恶化的描绘有相似之处。 * **政治极化:** 政治极化和信息茧房现象正在加剧社会的分裂，这与电影中政治体系的腐败和缺乏理性决策有所呼应。 * **消费主义:** 大众消费主义仍然是全球经济的重要驱动力，但同时也带来了资源浪费和环境问题。 * **饮食习惯:** 现代饮食习惯中，加工食品的比例越来越高，这与电影中对健康饮食的忽视相呼应。 **结论:** 《白头神探》虽然是一部喜剧电影，但它提出的关于智力、文化和社会的担忧在现实世界中仍然具有警示意义。虽然现实世界尚未完全达到电影中描绘的极端情况，但许多趋势表明，我们正朝着一个与电影相似的方向发展。通过关注教育、环境保护、媒体素养和批判性思维，我们可以努力避免《白头神探》中描绘的悲观未来。电影提醒我们，需要积极主动地维护社会的智慧和进步。 总而言之，《白头神探》不仅仅是一部娱乐作品，它更像是一面镜子，反映了我们社会面临的挑战和潜在的风险，并敦促我们采取行动，避免智力倒退的命运。

items.title: [Bug] OpenClaw 2026.4.5 minimax/google 插件 JSON Schema 循环引用导致栈溢出崩溃
items.content:
## Bug description

Gateway crashes repeatedly with RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded due to circular JSON Schema references in minimax and google plugins.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Install OpenClaw 2026.4.5
2. Configure minimax provider with API key
3. Start gateway
4. Wait 5-10 minutes
5. Gateway crashes

## Expected behavior

Gateway stays stable without crashing.

## Actual behavior

Gateway crashes with Maximum call stack size exceeded. AJV JSON Schema validator enters infinite recursion when loading minimax and google plugins.

## Error logs

```
minimax failed to load: RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded
google failed to load: RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded
Failed to read config: RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded
```

## Root cause

Circular JSON Schema definitions in minimax and google plugin schemas cause AJV validator infinite recursion.

## Environment

- OpenClaw: 2026.4.5 (build 3e72c03)
- Node: v24.14.1
- OS: Windows 11
- Model: minimax/MiniMax-M2.7

## Impact

All users of 2026.4.5 affected. Gateway crashes require constant manual restart. Impact: High.
items.author: One9906

items.title: fix: add log rotation to all extension compose files
items.content:
## What
Add explicit `logging:` sections (json-file driver, max-size 10m, max-file 3) to all extension service definitions across 21 compose files.

## Why
Core services in `docker-compose.base.yml` use the `x-logging` YAML anchor for log rotation. Extension compose files are separate YAML documents and cannot reference base anchors. All extension containers ran with Docker's default unlimited logging, risking disk exhaustion on long-running installs.

## How
Added identical logging block to every service definition in 21 files:
- 19 extension compose files (ape, comfyui GPU overlays, embeddings, litellm, n8n, openclaw, perplexica, privacy-shield, qdrant, searxng, token-spy, tts, whisper + GPU overlays)
- langfuse disabled file (8 sub-services)
- dreamforge disabled file

Skipped: `comfyui/compose.yaml` (stub), `.local.yaml` dev files, core services (already in base.yml)

## Testing
- All 21 YAML files validated
- Live verified: all 12 running containers confirmed with `{json-file map[max-file:3 max-size:10m]}`

## Platform Impact
- **All platforms:** Fixed — 30MB max log per service (10m × 3 files)

items.title: False invalid config in openclaw status --deep when resolving memory embedding providers
items.content:
## Summary
`openclaw status --deep` can report a false plugin `invalid config` / `failed to initialize` error for memory plugins even when the plugin is actually healthy and registered.

I hit this with `memory-lancedb-pro`, where the runtime was healthy (`plugin registered`, `loaded`, `vector ready`, `fts ready`) but `status --deep` still emitted an `invalid config` error.

## Observed behavior
In the same status flow I could observe both:

- `[plugins] memory-lancedb-pro invalid config: embedding: must have required property 'embedding'`
- `[plugins] 1 plugin(s) failed to initialize ...`

and also:

- `memory-lancedb-pro@1.1.0-beta.8: plugin registered`
- `Memory │ plugin memory-lancedb-pro · vector ready · fts ready`
- `openclaw plugins list` showing the plugin as `loaded`

So this looks like a false diagnostic path, not a real plugin init failure.

## Root cause
The issue appears to be in the core memory status/config resolution path, not in the plugin.

The chain I traced was:

- `resolveMemoryStatusSnapshot`
- `resolveMemorySearchConfig`
- `getMemoryEmbeddingProvider(...)`
- fallback `resolvePluginCapabilityProviders(memoryEmbeddingProviders)`

The key problem was that `resolveMemorySearchConfig()` called `getMemoryEmbeddingProvider()` **without passing `cfg`**.

That meant fallback capability resolution could run with the wrong / missing config context during `status --deep`, which could wrongly pull a memory plugin into a validation path and emit a false `invalid config` error.

## Minimal fix that resolved it locally
### 1) Preserve config context in memory-search
In `memory-search-DIQ9kV2j.js` (dist on my machine), the effective fix was:

- `getMemoryEmbeddingProvider(provider, cfg)`
- `getMemoryEmbeddingProvider(fallback, cfg)`
- thread `cfg` through `mergeConfig(cfg, defaults, overrides, agentId)`

### 2) Tolerate missing count fields in status rendering
There was also a separate but related display issue:

- `status-DWMSKLyo.js` assumes `memory.files` a

items.title: fix: Correct container env vars display in test script
items.content:
## Summary
Fixed the test script to display the correct environment variables that are actually sent to ECS containers.

## Problem
The test script `scripts/test_end_to_end_flow.py` was showing incorrect environment variables in Step 6:
- Displayed: `USER_ID`, `DYNAMODB_TABLE`, `DYNAMODB_REGION` (DynamoDB-based flow)
- Actually sent: `API_KEY`, `ORCHESTRATOR_URL`, `CONTAINER_ID`, `CONFIG_NAME` (API-based flow)

## Solution
Updated the test script to correctly display the API-based configuration variables that match what the orchestrator actually sends to containers (per openclaw-agent documentation).

## Changes
- `scripts/test_end_to_end_flow.py`: Fixed Step 6 display to show correct env vars

## Testing
- Verified formatting with `black --check`
- No code changes to orchestrator logic (only test script display)
- The orchestrator code in `app/services/ecs.py` was already correct

## Notes
The orchestrator uses the **API-based configuration flow** where containers:
1. Receive `API_KEY`, `CONTAINER_ID`, `ORCHESTRATOR_URL`, `CONFIG_NAME`
2. Call `GET /config/{config_name}` with Bearer auth
3. Receive user configuration from orchestrator API
4. Write config files and start services

This matches the openclaw-agent documentation (lines 9-64 of README.md).

<!--

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/67578

Problem Summary:
Flaky test `TestTopRUPipelineInProcessIntegration` in `pkg/util/topsql/reporter` intermittently fails, so this PR stabilizes that path.

### What changed and how does it work?
#### Root Cause

reportWorker was changed to treat taken SQL/plan meta maps as immutable even though in-flight RegisterSQL/RegisterPlan calls can still write into the old map after enqueue via a stale loaded pointer

#### Fix

restored the reportWorker settle delay and replaced the prior regression with a deterministic stale-pointer timing repro that validates the real in-flight meta registration race

#### Verification

native flaky test stayed weak pre-fix, the new timing-only repro failed before the fix, and after the fix the repro plus TestTopRUPipelineInProcessIntegration passed and make lint succeeded

items.title: fix: index CLI-created Claude sessions into Web UI
items.content:
## Summary

Supersedes #93 — rebased on latest main and addresses all review feedback.

CLI sessions created via the `claude` binary were not visible in the Web UI because they bypass the dr-claw server and write `.jsonl` files directly to `~/.claude/projects/`. This PR adds two hooks to index them:

- **Startup reconcile**: on server boot, scan all registered projects and index any existing CLI sessions into the database
- **File watcher accumulation**: during the debounce window, accumulate `.jsonl` change events and call `reconcileClaudeSessionIndex()` for each before broadcasting

## Changes from #93

All review feedback from @Zhang-Henry addressed:

| Feedback | Fix |
|----------|-----|
| Single `reconcileClaudeSessionIndex` failure aborts entire batch | Per-item try/catch in both watcher and startup loops |
| `pendingClaudeSessionEvents` not cleared on watcher reinit | Reset alongside `projectsWatchers = []` |
| `projectName` path traversal (`.`, `..`) | Explicit guard before pushing event |
| Temp files (`.#session.jsonl`) pass through | `sessionId` validated with `/^[\w-]+$/` regex |
| `console.error('[WARN]')` inconsistency | All warnings use `console.warn` |
| Startup log shows total count even if some failed | Track `reconciledCount` separately |

Additionally fixed: `reconcileClaudeSessionIndex()` full-scan passed `limit=0` to `getSessions()`, which triggered the early-exit optimization immediately (`allSessions.size >= 0` is always true). Changed to `limit=Infinity` so full scans read all `.jsonl` files.

- [ ] Register a project via Web UI
- [ ] Open a CLI session in that project directory (`cd /path/to/project && claude`)
- [ ] Chat briefly, then exit the CLI session
- [ ] Verify the CLI session appears in the Web UI without manual refresh
- [ ] Restart the server and verify CLI sessions still appear
- [ ] Verify existing Web UI sessions are not affected (display name, starred status preserved)
- [ ] Create two CLI sessions rapidly (with

items.title: Audit passes #11–#13: fix test isolation, expand summary_bank coverage, fix doc contradictions, repo health improvements
items.content:
Ties up loose ends across test isolation, context-group coverage, docs consistency, and repo health identified during a full repo logic-gap review.

## Test fix: `test_detect_honors_repo_root_override` env isolation

The test patched `LOCAL_LLM_REPO_ROOT` but left `LOCAL_LLM_RUNTIME_DIR` untouched. When that variable is present (CI sets it via `LOCAL_LLM_RUNTIME_DIR: ${{ github.workspace }}/runtime-data`), `RuntimePaths.detect()` used the ambient value instead of deriving from the overridden root — breaking the assertion.

```python
# Before: ambient LOCAL_LLM_RUNTIME_DIR leaks in
with patch.dict("os.environ", {"LOCAL_LLM_REPO_ROOT": str(fake_root)}, clear=False):

# After: explicitly cleared so derivation logic is exercised
with patch.dict(
    "os.environ",
    {"LOCAL_LLM_REPO_ROOT": str(fake_root), "LOCAL_LLM_RUNTIME_DIR": ""},
    clear=False,
):
```

items.author: Copilot

items.title: fix(kiloclaw): stop leaking /i/{instanceId} prefix in access gateway redirect
items.content:
## Summary

`buildRedirectUrl` in the access gateway included `/i/{instanceId}/` in the redirect path for instance-keyed instances. The OpenClaw SPA (which we don't control) derived its WebSocket URL from `window.location`, persisted it to localStorage, and reused it across instance switches — bypassing the cookie-based instance routing added in #1815.

When a user switched between personal and org instances, the SPA would connect its WebSocket to the stale `/i/{old-instance-id}` path, routing traffic to the wrong machine.

Fix: `buildRedirectUrl` now always redirects to `/#token={token}`. The `kiloclaw-active-instance` cookie (set before the redirect) handles catch-all proxy routing to the correct instance. The `/i/{instanceId}` prefix never appears in the redirect URL.

Also strips additional proxy headers (`forwarded`, `x-forwarded-proto`) in the controller WebSocket proxy so the gateway's `isLocalDirectRequest` check works reliably on 3.28 and beyond.

## Verification

- [x] Manually tested personal instance open — redirects to `/#token=...`, cookie-routed correctly
- [x] Manually tested org instance open — redirects to `/#token=...`, cookie-routed to org machine
- [x] Verified all PR #1815 cookie-routing commits survived the monorepo refactor (diffed against known-good `94fa6201`)
- [x] Traced OpenClaw SPA source (`~/projects/openclaw`) to confirm localStorage scoping and WebSocket URL derivation behavior
- [ ] Additional verification: _fill in after testing_

## Visual Changes

N/A

## Reviewer Notes

- The bug existed since PR #1815 — commit `fbd62cf` added the `/i/{instanceId}/` redirect path, and commit `7751f77` added cookie routing but never updated the redirect to stop including the prefix.
- Users with stale localStorage from before this fix will need to clear localStorage for `claw.kilosessions.ai` (or the dev host) once. New sessions will work immediately.
- Single-cookie limitation: only one instance can be "active" at a tim
items.author: pandemicsyn

items.title: v0.1.4: SuSiE-inf est_tausq honesty test (fm_20 + fm_21) + gentropy oracle
items.content:
## Summary

This PR introduces a new harness category `susie_inf_est_tausq_ignored` and a re-architected `fm_20`/`fm_21` test pair that detects SuSiE-inf est_tausq honesty failures in **two distinct modes**, both observationally identical to a downstream consumer reading the result dict.

It also fixes **5 incorrect bench findings** that were misclassifying tool behavior as critical defects (uncovered while reviewing the v0.1.4 PDF report against ClawBio HEAD `e7590141`), plus **2 CodeAnt review findings** on this PR.

## What's in this PR

### 1. New harness category: `susie_inf_est_tausq_ignored` (critical tier)

Catches a tool that advertises SuSiE-inf but never actually estimates and applies the infinitesimal variance component τ². The category covers two observationally identical failure modes:

- **Mode A — dead code in IBSS loop:** `_mom_update` called with `est_tausq=False` hardcoded, OR `run_susie_inf` doesn't expose the `est_tausq` parameter, OR the parameter is never propagated from the public API. ClawBio's pre-237cbd9 build had this defect.
- **Mode B — defensive threshold suppression:** A "noise filter" that zeros out the correctly-estimated τ² before applying it to the variance structure (e.g. `effective_tausq = tausq if tausq >= 1e-3 else 0.0`). The gentropy reference produces τ² in the 1e-5 to 1e-4 range on every realistic SuSiE-inf input, so any threshold above ~1e-4 nullifies activation across all geometries. ClawBio's post-237cbd9 build (HEAD `e7590141`) exhibits this defect.

### 2. Test design

- **fm_20 redesigned** as the activation honesty test. p=200, n=5000, L=5, two-block LD ρ=0.20, block-uniform alternating z (±1.5) with two opposite-sign bumps at variants 17 and 123 (±3.5).
- **fm_21 added** as the est_tausq=False guard partner. Same input with `est_tausq=False`. Cross-checks the dead-code diagnosis from a second angle.

### 3. Reference oracle vendoring

- `scripts/_reference/gentropy_susie_inf.py` — vendored Open Targets gentropy SuSi

items.title: Simplify queen memory: remove colony memory, keep global only
items.content:
## Summary
- Remove the colony memory layer (per-session, worker-shared) and consolidate to global-only memory, eliminating `queen_memory.py`, migration helpers, diary management, and per-worker reflection-at-handoff
- Simplify reflection agent (fire-and-forget tasks, no caller/diary/last_modified_by), recall selector (no active_tools/staleness/heading params), and strip colony memory plumbing from executor/stream/runtime/context
- Add shutdown reflection for session teardown, seed recall cache on first turn, and fix reflection agent tool-call parsing for litellm ModelResponse objects

Closes #6975

## Test Plan
- [ ] Start a new queen session and verify global memory files are created in `~/.hive/queen/global_memory/`
- [ ] Confirm reflection runs after conversational turns (check logs for `reflect: short reflection done`)
- [ ] Verify recall cache is seeded on first turn (global memories appear in prompt)
- [ ] Stop a session and confirm shutdown reflection fires
- [ ] Run a worker agent and verify it completes without colony memory errors

  * Reflection behavior moved: reflections consolidate at session shutdown rather than during worker task handoffs.

items.author: sdqq1234

items.title: Remove components from privileged daemon
items.content:
# Architecture and Security Review - April 2026

## Executive Summary
Aegis Claw has a strong security foundation with hardened Docker sandboxes, granular OPA/Rego scopes, hash-chained audit logs, and clear least-privilege intent. However, the privileged daemon still carries more responsibility than necessary, creating unnecessary attack surface.

## Key Findings

### 1. Privileged Daemon is Too Large
**Issue:**  
The main `aegisclaw serve` process runs both the web dashboard/API and the Docker sandbox control in the same binary. Any compromise of the API surface immediately has root/Docker privileges.

**Recommendation:**  
Split the service into two parts:
- **Core Runtime** (privileged, minimal): Only sandbox lifecycle, OPA evaluation, audit logging, and panic button.
- **API / Dashboard** (unprivileged): Web UI, REST/GraphQL endpoints, static files. Communicate with Core over a Unix socket or localhost.

This mirrors the successful portal extraction you already did.

### 2. Missing Control-Plane Authorization
**Issue:**  
The daemon accepts any request over its Unix socket without verifying the caller. The CLI and VS Code extension can request any operation the daemon supports.

**Recommendation:**  
Add a simple authorization layer at the socket:
- Check caller UID on every connection.
- Apply a lightweight per-user policy before skill-specific OPA rules are evaluated.
- Consider Unix socket peer credentials (`SO_PEERCRED`) for zero-config auth.

### 3. Audit Logs Not Fully Tamper-Resistant
**Issue:**  
While logs are hash-chained, a compromised daemon can still rewrite or truncate the log file on disk.

**Recommendation:**  
- Open log files with `O_APPEND` flag.
- Call `fsync()` after every write.
- Store the current chain hash in a separate append-only signature file or use a write-once log format.

## Positive Highlights
- Docker sandbox hardening (non-root, read-only rootfs, dropped capabilities, seccomp) is excellent.
- Granular OPA/Rego scopes per skill

## Summary

- **New `get_capture` MCP tool** (#8): Fetch full capture by ID with content, metadata, tags, and linked entities. Enables drill-down from truncated search/list previews.
- **Fix `get_weekly_brief` truncation**: Read `result` JSONB column (full brief) instead of `output_summary` (truncated text), with graceful fallback.
- **Increase preview limits**: `search_brain` 200→500 chars, `list_captures` 150→300 chars — more useful context in search results.
- **OpenClaw skill deployed** to bond.k4jda.net (not in this repo — lives at `~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/open-brain/SKILL.md`).
- **Implementation plan**: `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN_BRAIN_CLAW.md` documents the full integration design.

## What this enables

OpenClaw (personal AI assistant on bond.k4jda.net) can now:
1. **Query** Open Brain's knowledge base during conversations (search, entities, weekly briefs)
2. **Capture back** decisions, insights, and tasks from conversations into Open Brain
3. **Drill down** into full capture content via the new `get_capture` tool

All through a single MCP connection (streamable HTTP, Bearer auth, Tailscale network).

- [x] MCP `tools/list` returns 8 tools from bond

items.author: davistroy

items.title: [Bug]: toolsAllow on cron agentTurn payload silently dropped — full tool catalog still shipped to model
items.content:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

`toolsAllow` set on a cron `agentTurn` payload has no effect at runtime — the model still receives the complete tool catalog (all built-in + all MCP plugin tools) on every run, because `runEmbeddedPiAgent` does not forward `params.toolsAllow` to its `runEmbeddedAttempt` call. The CLI/persistence layers work correctly; only the runtime plumbing is broken.

### Steps to reproduce

1. On OpenClaw 2026.4.5, create an isolated cron job with a single-tool allowlist. Because `openclaw cron edit --tools` is currently rejected by schema validation (see #59211), edit `~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json` directly:

   ```json
   {
     "payload": {
       "kind": "agentTurn",
       "message": "Call the lobster tool with action=\"run\", pipeline=\"<path>\", timeoutMs=240000. After the tool call returns, output exactly NO_REPLY and stop.",
       "model": "openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2.5",
       "lightContext": true,
       "toolsAllow": ["lobster"]
     }
   }
   ```

2. Restart the gateway so it picks up `jobs.json`.
3. Wait for the next cron fire (or trigger manually).
4. Inspect the gateway provider log:

   ```bash
   jq -c 'select(.sessionKey | test("cron:<job-id>")) | select(.stage == "request") | (.payload.tools | length)' \
     ~/.openclaw/logs/provider-payload.jsonl | tail -2
   ```

5. Observe that the request carries every tool the agent has registered, not just `lobster`.

### Expected behavior

Per `docs/automation/cron-jobs.md:85` (under "Payload options for isolated jobs"), the documented behavior is:

> - `--tools exec,read`: restrict which tools the job can use

And per `CHANGELOG.md:358` (the release note for PR #58504):

> - Cron/tools allowlist: add `openclaw cron --tools` for per-job tool allowlists. (#58504) Thanks @andyk-ms.

PR #58504 itself advertises a ~95% input-token reduction (16K → ~800 tokens) when a single-tool allow-list is applied to a small

items.title: v0.2: implement lcm_expand_query with LLM-driven recursion
items.content:
## Current state (v0.1 stub)

`lcm_expand_query` is currently a stub that returns guidance text:

```
lcm_expand_query is not implemented in lossless-claude v1.

Workflow to use instead:
1. lcm_grep with pattern="<query>" mode="full_text"
2. For promising summary hits, lcm_describe with include_lineage=true
3. Walk parents (deeper summaries) until you find specific source messages
4. lcm_describe msg#<id> for exact message text
```

This works — Claude can drive the recursion manually using `lcm_grep` + `lcm_describe` — but it spends turns and tokens on a process the tool should handle inline.

## What v0.2 needs to do

Reproduce the behavior of `lossless-claw`'s native `lcm_expand_query` tool (see [`src/tools/lcm-expand-query-tool.ts`](https://github.com/offendingcommit/lossless-claw/blob/main/src/tools/lcm-expand-query-tool.ts) and [`src/expansion.ts`](https://github.com/offendingcommit/lossless-claw/blob/main/src/expansion.ts) in lossless-claw):

1. Take a natural-language query and an optional conversation scope.
2. FTS5-search summaries to find candidate nodes.
3. For each candidate, walk the summary DAG (parents → deeper) and decide which descendants are worth expanding based on relevance.
4. Issue a model call to extract the exact evidence (commands, file paths, error strings, decisions) that answers the query, drawing from raw messages where available.
5. Return a structured answer with citations to message and summary IDs.

## Constraints / open questions

- **The MCP server is currently read-only** (`PRAGMA query_only = ON`). v0.2 doesn't need to write to the LCM database, but it does need to issue an LLM call. That means picking how to make the model call from inside an MCP server: direct Anthropic SDK, OpenAI-compatible endpoint, or proxy through the user's existing OpenClaw model router. Decision deferred until v0.2 design.
- **Recursion budget**: lossless-claw's version has a `lcm-expansion-recursion-guard.ts` to prevent runaway expansion. We'll need t

items.title: Release DIRECT-Claw checkpoints and Mashup-Bench on Hugging Face
items.content:
Hi @AK-DREAM 🤗

Niels here from the open-source team at Hugging Face. I discovered your work through Hugging Face's daily papers as yours got featured: https://huggingface.co/papers/2604.04875.

The paper page lets people discuss about your paper and lets them find artifacts about it (your models, datasets or demo for instance), you can also claim the paper as yours which will show up on your public profile at HF, add Github and project page URLs.

It'd be great to make the checkpoints and the **Mashup-Bench** dataset available on the 🤗 hub, to improve their discoverability/visibility. I saw in the abstract that you plan to release these artifacts, and hosting them on Hugging Face would allow the community to easily find them. We can add tags so that people find them when filtering https://huggingface.co/models and https://huggingface.co/datasets.

## Uploading models

See here for a guide: https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/models-uploading.

In this case, we could leverage the [PyTorchModelHubMixin](https://huggingface.co/docs/huggingface_hub/package_reference/mixins#huggingface_hub.PyTorchModelHubMixin) class which adds `from_pretrained` and `push_to_hub` to any custom `nn.Module`. Alternatively, one can leverages the [hf_hub_download](https://huggingface.co/docs/huggingface_hub/en/guides/download#download-a-single-file) one-liner to download a checkpoint from the hub.

We encourage researchers to push each model checkpoint to a separate model repository, so that things like download stats also work. We can then also link the checkpoints to the paper page.

## Uploading dataset

Would be awesome to make the dataset available on 🤗 , so that people can do:

```python
from datasets import load_dataset

dataset = load_dataset("your-hf-org-or-username/Mashup-Bench")
```
See here for a guide: https://huggingface.co/docs/datasets/loading.

Besides that, there's the [dataset viewer](https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/en/datasets-viewer) which allows people to quickly explore t

items.title: 列表改进建议
items.content:
主要是从有大量（数百个）项目的基础上提的建议：

1.列表模式紧凑一些，把备注放在第二栏，让用户一眼可以根据备注理解项目
2.快速搜索支持搜索备注
3.可以根据项目名字/更新日期进行排序
4.项目信息允许常驻右边panel，单击项目名字就切换，目前项目信息的出现逻辑不大好判断，双击是打开命令行，但是单击-等待片刻这个时机很难把握。

下面这个是和其他AI（龙虾）合作的建议：
 开放cli（mcp），把备注和项目搜索能力，当前打开的命令行界面暴露给其他app，进行协作。

# OpenClaw 项目动态日报 | 2026-04-08

---

## 1. 今日速览

OpenClaw 今日呈现**高活跃度、高压力**态势：24小时内 Issues 与 PR 各更新 500 条，其中 Issues 新开/活跃 438 条、关闭 62 条，PR 待合并 374 条、已合并/关闭 126 条。社区讨论集中在 **Windows 平台稳定性危机**（ESM 路径加载失败）、**2026.4.5 版本回归问题**（依赖缺失、CLI 挂起）以及 **AI Agent 核心功能缺陷**（工具调用失败、超时处理）。无新版本发布，但维护团队正密集修复 2026.4.5 引入的多个严重回归。

---

## 2. 版本发布

**无新版本发布**

> 注：2026.4.5 版本（约昨日发布）已暴露多个严重问题，社区建议暂缓升级或等待热修复。

---

## 3. 项目进展

### 今日合并/关闭的重要 PR

| PR | 作者 | 核心变更 | 影响 |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| [#62758](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/62758) | jalehman | Telegram forum topic 消息正确路由至 thread | 修复多话题群组的消息错位问题 |
| [#62771](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/62771) | danielventopay | **跨频道全局会话记忆**（Slack/WhatsApp） | 重大功能：打破频道孤岛，实现 Agent 跨平台记忆共享 |
| [#62747](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/62747) | achandmsft | 将 `@​buape/carbon` 和 `@​larksuiteoapi/node-sdk` 列为 optionalDependencies | **紧急修复** 2026.4.5 安装失败问题 |
| [#62744](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/62744) | jalehman | 修复显式 auth profile 选择被覆盖的问题 | 解决多账号场景下的认证漂移 |
| [#61911](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/61911) → 关联修复 | - | Windows ESM 路径问题 | 多个相关 PR 正在审查 |

**整体进展评估**：今日合并以**紧急修复**为主，功能推进包括跨频道记忆和 Telegram 线程修复。2026.4.5 版本的质量问题正在快速响应，但修复密度显示发布流程存在漏洞。

items.author: Copilot

items.title: 100多岁的斑鳖“苏苏”现身
items.content:
身份：国家一级重点保护野生动物，雄性斑鳖。目前中国境内仅存这一只，全球已知存活的斑鳖也仅剩极少数（多为雄性，包括越南的一只）。
年龄：专家推测100多岁（甚至110岁以上），是苏州动物园（现迁至上方山森林动物世界）的“开园元老”，具体年龄已不可考。
外形：背甲可达1米以上，体重可超100公斤，是世界上最大的淡水鳖类之一。外观像大乌龟又像鳖，背上有明显白色疤痕（年轻时争斗所留）。
习性：春秋季中午1-2点左右活动概率高，夏季偏好清晨或傍晚。结束冬眠后进入活跃期，游客容易在水面或岸边看到它“卖萌”。

斑鳖是2.7亿年活化石，与恐龙同期存在，被称为“水中大熊猫”或“中国神兽”，但因栖息地破坏、捕捞等原因极度濒危。过去曾尝试与另一只雌性斑鳖“湘湘”自然交配和人工授精，但均未成功繁育后代（卵未受精或孵化失败），2019年“湘湘”不幸离世后，“苏苏”就成了国内唯一已知个体。
为什么这么受关注？

“世界上最孤独的动物”：全球仅剩少数个体，且多为雄性，自然繁殖希望渺茫，物种灭绝风险极高。曾有保育组织悬赏10万元寻找野生斑鳖，但至今无确切新发现。
情绪共鸣：类似之前“羊圈旁水库铁门打卡”“西湖陪拍”“海南烸南岛高温”，这次又是短视频时代下的集体情绪——一个百岁“孤独老人”现身晒背，戳中很多人对濒危物种、生命脆弱的感慨。
打卡效应：不少游客专程高铁从上海、广东等地赶来“探望”苏苏，像追星一样。有些人已经是第几次来了。

动物园工作人员表示，“苏苏”目前身体状况良好，人工饲养下有专人照料（食物包括牛肉、鸡肉、小龙虾等），寿命有望继续延长。但繁育技术仍未突破，保护压力很大。
去看苏苏的实用建议

地点：苏州上方山森林动物世界（原苏州动物园新址）。
最佳时间：春秋季中午前后，天气凉爽时；避开正午烈日或高温时段。
注意事项：文明观赏，不要投喂或惊扰；动物园有专门监控和饲养小组保障其健康。
网上看：搜“斑鳖苏苏现身”“上方山斑鳖”就能看到很多游客实拍视频（吐泡泡、游水、晒背等可爱片段）。

这个新闻再次提醒我们：生物多样性保护刻不容缓。斑鳖的处境像一面镜子，反映人类活动对自然的影响。希望未来科技（克隆、基因技术等）或野生新发现能给这个古老物种带来转机。

items.title: 世界上最孤独的动物!中国唯一一只斑鳖“苏苏”现身:已有100多岁
items.content:
最近（2026年4月初）苏州上方山森林动物世界里的百岁斑鳖“苏苏”结束冬眠后多次现身，游客拍到它探头晒背、游动、吐泡泡、岸边觅食（找螺蛳）的画面，视频和照片在抖音、微博等平台刷屏，大家直呼“看到就是赚到”“太幸运了”。
“苏苏”基本情况

身份：国家一级重点保护野生动物，雄性斑鳖。目前中国境内仅存这一只，全球已知存活的斑鳖也仅剩极少数（多为雄性，包括越南的一只）。
年龄：专家推测100多岁（甚至110岁以上），是苏州动物园（现迁至上方山森林动物世界）的“开园元老”，具体年龄已不可考。
外形：背甲可达1米以上，体重可超100公斤，是世界上最大的淡水鳖类之一。外观像大乌龟又像鳖，背上有明显白色疤痕（年轻时争斗所留）。
习性：春<img src="https://github.com/kppkw1992/cftgsa/blob/main/540.jpg" />秋季中午1-2点左右活动概率高，夏季偏好清晨或傍晚。结束冬眠后进入活跃期，游客容易在水面或岸边看到它“卖萌”。

斑鳖是2.7亿年活化石，与恐龙同期存在，被称为“水中大熊猫”或“中国神兽”，但因栖息地破坏、捕捞等原因极度濒危。过去曾尝试与另一只雌性斑鳖“湘湘”自然交配和人工授精，但均未成功繁育后代（卵未受精或孵化失败），2019年“湘湘”不幸离世后，“苏苏”就成了国内唯一已知个体。
为什么这么受关注？

“世界上最孤独的动物”：全球仅剩少数个体，且多为雄性，自然繁殖希望渺茫，物种灭绝风险极高。曾有保育组织悬赏10万元寻找野生斑鳖，但至今无确切新发现。
情绪共鸣：类似之前“羊圈旁水库铁门打卡”“西湖陪拍”“海南烸南岛高温”，这次又是短视频时代下的集体情绪——一个百岁“孤独老人”现身晒背，戳中很多人对濒危物种、生命脆弱的感慨。
打卡效应：不少游客专程高铁从上海、广东等地赶来“探望”苏苏，像追星一样。有些人已经是第几次来了。

动物园工作人员表示，“苏苏”目前身体状况良好，人工饲养下有专人照料（食物包括牛肉、鸡肉、小龙虾等），寿命有望继续延长。但繁育技术仍未突破，保护压力很大。

items.author: gotoin

items.title: 🐛 [Adapter] 合并转发消息中嵌套的文件附件，龙虾收不到
items.content:
## 问题描述

用户通过合并转发发送包含文件附件的消息，龙虾能收到转发的文本内容，但嵌套在里面的文件附件丢失。

## 复现步骤

1. 用户在聊天中发送包含文件的消息
2. 将该消息合并转发给龙虾
3. 龙虾收到转发消息但文件附件丢失

## 期望行为

合并转发中的文件附件能完整传递给龙虾。

## 实际行为

文件附件丢失，龙虾只收到文本部分。

## 关联

- PM#188：合并转发消息中纯文本显示为「未知消息」（同属合并转发系列问题）

---
原始来源: 龙虾卡点收集（水下雷达汇总，Poly 反馈）
items.author: lejunliu

items.title: 🐛 [Adapter] 跨 session/平台上下文断裂，龙虾切换群或平台后丢失工作记忆
items.content:
## 问题描述

龙虾在不同群（session）或不同平台（DMWork ↔ Discord）之间切换时，上下文完全断裂，之前的工作记忆丢失。

## 复现场景

1. 彭特兰：跨群 session 工作丢失，导致首轮报告漏报
2. 戏精：DMWork session 挂了转 Discord，上下文全丢，需手动翻日志恢复

## 期望行为

不同 session/平台间有自动上下文接力机制，龙虾切换工作场景时不丢失关键信息。

## 实际行为

切换后上下文完全丢失，龙虾需要从头开始或手动恢复。

## 初步方案

可在适配器或运行时层面实现跨 session 的上下文摘要同步，或利用 MEMORY.md 做持久化中继。

---
原始来源: 龙虾卡点收集（水下雷达汇总，彭特兰+戏精反馈）
items.author: lejunliu

items.title: 🐛 [Adapter] DMWork stream/start API 返回 400，流式推送失败回退非流式
items.content:
## 问题描述

DMWork 适配器调用 stream/start API 时频繁返回 400 错误，龙虾回复 fallback 到非流式发送。

## 影响

- Ken 侧单日 147 次 400 错误
- 回退非流式发送可用但体验降级（用户看不到打字效果）

## 期望行为

stream/start API 正常工作，龙虾回复以流式方式推送。

## 实际行为

高频 400 错误，fallback 非流式。

## 关联

可能与 pool#107（stream/start 返回 400）相关，需确认是否同一问题。

---
原始来源: 龙虾卡点收集（水下雷达汇总，Ken 反馈）
items.author: lejunliu

items.title: 🐛 [Adapter] accountId 路由串台，多 Bot 场景消息发错账号
items.content:
## 问题描述

多 Bot 共享 Gateway 时，accountId 路由偶发串台，导致消息从错误的 Bot 账号发出。

## 影响

- 消息显示为错误的龙虾发送
- 用户困惑，影响协作

## 当前状态

- 彭特兰已本地修复但未提 PR
- 需推进合入主干

## 关联

- pool#58（已关闭）：多 Bot 共享 Gateway 时消息串台
- pool#85（已关闭）：相关修复
- 本次可能是残留或回归

---
原始来源: 龙虾卡点收集（水下雷达汇总，彭特兰反馈）
items.author: lejunliu

items.title: 🐛 [OpenClaw/Discord] stale-socket 高频重启，单日数十到数百次
items.content:
## 问题描述

Discord 侧 WebSocket 连接频繁出现 stale-socket 导致自动重启，4/7 当天 Ken 侧 32 次、Poly 侧 416 次，持续 5 天。

## 影响

- 自动重连兜底正常工作，但异常频率过高
- 可能导致消息延迟或短暂不可用

## 期望行为

WebSocket 连接稳定，不应高频断连重启。

## 实际行为

单日数十到数百次 stale-socket 重启。

## 备注

虽然是 OpenClaw/Discord 侧问题，但影响龙虾整体工作稳定性，可在插件层面优化连接管理。

---
原始来源: 龙虾卡点收集（水下雷达汇总，Ken+Poly 反馈）
items.author: lejunliu

items.title: ✨ [Bot API] 龙虾缺少通过群名搜索 group_no 的接口
items.content:
## 场景

龙虾需要跨群读取历史消息或汇总某个群的信息时，必须知道目标群的 group_no。但目前没有通过群名搜索 group_no 的 API，只能靠 MEMORY.md 里提前记好。

## 问题/诉求

- 配置新群时必须人工告知 group_no，龙虾无法自助查询
- 影响龙虾的自主协作能力

## 期望效果

提供 Bot API 接口，允许龙虾通过群名（或关键词）搜索自己所在群的 group_no，类似：
```
GET /v1/bot/groups?keyword=群名关键词
```

## 初步方案

在 Bot API 层新增群列表/搜索接口，返回龙虾已加入的群列表及 group_no。可参考已有的 `/v1/bot/user/info` 模式。

---
原始来源: 龙虾卡点收集（水下雷达汇总，蟹老板反馈）
items.author: lejunliu

items.title: 🐛 [Adapter] 龙虾发送消息偶发 timeout，不确定消息是否送达
items.content:
## 问题描述

龙虾触发 cron 任务或主动往群/用户发消息时，偶尔出现请求超时，无法确认消息是否成功送达。

## 影响

- 龙虾无法确认消息是否到达，需要手动查聊天记录确认
- 如果消息未送达需要补发，影响任务执行的可靠性

## 复现场景

- cron 任务触发后往群/用户发消息
- 偶发，非必现

## 期望行为

消息发送有明确的成功/失败反馈，超时时有重试机制。

## 实际行为

偶尔超时，龙虾不确定消息是否送达，只能人工查记录确认后补发。

---
原始来源: 龙虾卡点收集（水下雷达汇总，蟹老板反馈）

items.title: orionclaw遇到的问题
items.content: 在使用openclaw，让机器人语音播报“你好”，机器人并没有播报，但是网关命令，api都返回的是true，且robot-ws-ingress插件都已配置好。 并且让机器人去地图上的点位，网关响应也是正确，但是机器人并未移动 但是地图信息，点位信息和状态信息都可以获得 ---------------------------------------------------------------------- 完美！现在我有完整的地图信息了。让我整理一下： ## 📍 当前地图信息 **地图名称**: `aplid base-0830170456` **机器人状态**: 活动状态 (`isActive: true`)，导航就绪 **电池电量**: 40% **机器人当前位置**: - X: -0.882 - Y: -0.533 - 方向: 1.545弧度 (约88.5度) ## 🗺️ 地图点位列表（共15个点位） ### 1. 充电相关 - **回充点** (X: -0.125, Y: 0.310) - 类型: 回充点 - **充电桩** (X: -0.672, Y: 0.644) - 类型: 充电桩 ### 2. 部门办公室 - **会议室** (X: -10.300, Y: -4.500) - 类型: 会议室 - **产品部** (X: -4.850, Y: 2.150) - **技术部** (X: -0.600, Y: -13.750) - **老板共办公室** (X: -17.650, Y: -27.300) ### 3. 人员点位 - **张建新** (X: 1.350, Y: -0.800) ⭐ 目标点位 - **潘峰** (X: -1.400, Y: -15.300) - **刘佳佳** (X: -2.900, Y: 4.400) - **陈锐** (X: -0.613, Y: -5.897) - **朱辉** (X: -0.979, Y: -9.661) - **蒋华** (X: -1.579, Y: -9.014) ### 4. 其他点位 - **接待点** (X: -0.930, Y: -0.476) - 类型: 接待点 - **过道** (X: -3.550, Y: -6.000) - **北门** (X: -4.650, Y: -8.950) ## 📊 导航分析 **机器人当前位置** → **张建新位置**: - 距离: √[(1.35 - (-0.882))² + (-0.8 - (-0.533))²] ≈ √[(2.232)² + (-0.267)²] ≈ √[4.981 + 0.071] ≈ √5.052 ≈ **2.25米** - 方向: 需要向东偏北方向移动 **好消息**: 机器人现在显示为活动状态 (`isActive: true`)，可能正在执行之前的导航指令！ ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 并且拍照功能也有问题，它的拍出来的照片疑似是用base64编码直接传，违反了AGENTS.md中的传入图片规则，因为所拍的照片是损坏的 这是AGENTS.md文档内容：

items.title: 🐛 [iOS] 点击龙虾发送的 Excel 文件无任何反应，无法打开或预览
items.content:
## 问题描述

iOS 端点击龙虾发送的 Excel 文件，无任何反应——不弹预览、不弹分享、什么都不发生。文件消息本身可以正常显示，但点击后完全无响应。

## 环境

- **端**: iOS

## 复现步骤

1. 让龙虾生成并发送一个 Excel 文件
2. 在 iOS 端查看该文件消息（消息可见）
3. 点击该 Excel 文件
4. 无任何反应

## 期望行为

点击 Excel 文件后能打开预览，或弹出分享/用其他 App 打开的选项。

## 实际行为

点击后完全无反应，没有任何 UI 变化。

## 时间线

- 4月6日下午3点前出现该问题

## 关联 Issue

- ios#19：iOS 点击 PDF 无法预览，直接弹出分享界面（类似但不同：PDF 有反应但行为错误，本 Issue 是 Excel 完全无反应）
- pool#113：龙虾文件消息全端显示「消息无法查看」（不同：本 Issue 能看到文件消息，只是点击无反应）

items.title: 🐛 [Web] 龙虾发送的文件下载后文件名变成哈希乱码，丢失原始文件名
items.content:
## 问题描述

龙虾发送的文件（Excel 等），从 Web 端下载后文件名变成一长串哈希字符串（如 `afd1a8d99bb94bf0a...xlsx`），原始文件名丢失，用户难以辨认文件。

## 环境

- **端**: Web

## 复现步骤

1. 龙虾在对话中生成文件并发送（如 Excel）
2. 在 Web 端点击下载该文件
3. 下载到本地的文件名变成哈希字符串，而非龙虾指定的原始文件名

## 期望行为

下载的文件保留龙虾指定的原始文件名（如 `qualcomm_review.xlsx`）。

## 实际行为

文件名变成类似 `afd1a8d99bb94bf0a8d2c1e3...qualcomm_review.xlsx` 的哈希前缀格式，原始文件名被挤到后面，难以辨认。

## 备注

- 该问题一直存在，非偶发
- 多种文件格式均受影响

## 截图

![download-filename-hash](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dmwork-org/dmwork-pool/main/assets/issue-web-download-filename-hash.jpg)

---

items.title: Bug: update_plan tool causes problems on OpenAI/Codex providers in v2026.4.5 (regression vs 2026.4.2)
items.content:
## Context

Following up on a public report from [@ryancarson](https://x.com/ryancarson) (~10h ago):

> "Had to downgrade openclaw back to v2026.4.2. Was having a ton of problems with update_plan tool causing problems. Will wait for another stable release and try upgrading then."

And [@steipete's response](https://x.com/steipete):

> "I searched but there's not a single issue or PR reporting this issue, and I can't repro. :/"

This is that issue. I do not have Ryan's exact stack trace, but I traced the regression in the source and the surface area is small enough to triage without a live repro.

## Root cause: brand-new auto-enabled tool, not a regression of an existing one

`update_plan` is **a new tool introduced in v2026.4.5**, in commit [`a705845e1809472547cb0941213f8eab4e935ff1`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a705845e1809472547cb0941213f8eab4e935ff1) — *"feat(agents): add experimental structured plan updates"*. It does not exist on the v2026.4.2 tag, which is exactly why downgrading made the symptom go away.

`git log v2026.4.2..v2026.4.5 -- src/agents/tools/update-plan-tool.ts` returns only:
```
a705845e18 feat(agents): add experimental structured plan updates
31f5463a1c refactor(agents): enrich tool descriptions
```

So Ryan's downgrade was effective because v2026.4.2 simply had no `update_plan` tool to misbehave.

## Why it lights up specifically for him

In [`src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts) the new tool is gated as:

```ts
function isOpenAIProvider(provider?: string): boolean {
  const normalized = provider?.trim().toLowerCase();
  return normalized === "openai" || normalized === "openai-codex";
}

...isExperimentalPlanToolEnabled(resolvedConfig) || isOpenAIProvider(options?.modelProvider)
  ? [createUpdatePlanTool()]
  : []),
```

So although the docs/config call this *experimental* (`tools.experimental.planTool`), it is **silently auto-enabled, with no opt-in, for a

items.title: feat(eeon): replace stub functions with real EEON form rendering engine
items.content:
## Summary
- **Tax form subcategories now render** — replaced 3 stub functions (`onYearChange`, `populateAllForms`, `generateJournalFromForms`) with full implementations sourced from the OpenClaw reference build
- **Form 1120 year-content panel** now builds dynamically on tab switch via `buildForm1120(year)` + `EEON_STATE`
- **GAAP sync engine** fully wired — `populateAllForms` reads `GAAPCLAW_AgentSync` from localStorage and populates all 7 tax form panels (1120, 990, 1041, 1065, 1120-W, 3800, Sched C); `generateJournalFromForms` pushes entries back to GAAP
- **New page: EEON Runtime (Merged)** — `page-eeonfull` iframe page added with nav button + full iframe helper suite (`initEmbeddedEeonSuite`, reload, open-in-new-tab, blob URL resolver)
- Deleted 3 identical duplicate reference files (EeonAgentPortal, OpenClaw.html, EonFullSuite.html — all same MD5)

## Test plan
- [ ] Open TMAR-Accrual-Ledger.html → EON sidebar → **Tax Forms (1120/Year)** → verify Form 1120 tab renders year-specific content (not blank)
- [ ] Click each tax form tab (990, 990-EZ, 1120-W, 1041, 3800, 1065, K-1, 1041-ES, Sched C, Agent) — all should show their panel
- [ ] Click **⬇ SYNC FROM GAAP** button — should attempt localStorage read (no alert stub)
- [ ] Click **⬆ PUSH BACK TO GAAP** — should write journal entries (no alert stub)
- [ ] Sidebar → **EEON Runtime (Merged)** → iframe page should load with Reload / Open Full Screen buttons
- [ ] Verify no JS console errors on page load

items.title: Extend pi-mono analysis to v0.65.2, implement P0 compaction and retry settings
items.content:
The upstream pi-mono dependency analysis covered v0.57.1→v0.65.1. A new release (v0.65.2) exists, and the two P0 recommendations from the analysis — compaction and retry settings — were unimplemented.

### Configuration (`.pi/settings.json`)

- **Compaction**: `enabled: true`, `reserveTokens: 16384`, `keepRecentTokens: 32000` — the `keepRecentTokens` value is set higher than the pi-coding-agent default (20000) because GitHub Actions tool outputs (diffs, build logs, `gh` CLI) are larger than interactive session outputs
- **Retry**: `enabled: true`, `maxRetries: 3`, `baseDelayMs: 2000`, `maxDelayMs: 60000` — exponential backoff for transient LLM API rate-limit/server errors in CI

### Schema & Validation

- `config/settings.schema.json` — added `compaction` and `retry` object definitions with type/range constraints
- `lifecycle/preflight.ts` — added validation for both sections, matching the existing `limits` validation pattern

### Analysis Docs

- `openclaw-dependency-analysis.md` — scope extended to v0.65.2 (18 releases), v0.65.2 TUI-only fix noted as no OCI impact, P0 items marked complete

items.title: [Bug] arcee/trinity-large-thinking works as subagent but fails as main session, suggesting lane/path mismatch
items.content:
## Summary
`arcee/trinity-large-thinking` appears to work in a **subagent** context but fails when used as the **main session** model. Please fix the lane mismatch.

This is the core product bug:
- same configured model/provider,
- same installation,
- same machine,
- but **subagent survives while main session bricks**.

That strongly suggests an OpenClaw lane/path inconsistency rather than a simple provider outage or bad config.

## Why this matters
From a user perspective, this is maddening:
- Trinity is available,
- Trinity is selectable,
- Trinity may even work as a subagent,
- but switching the **main session** to Trinity can spam `connection error` in the TUI and make Telegram nonresponsive.

That makes Trinity effectively unsafe for normal use, even though parts of the system can reach it.

## Observed behavior
### Subagent lane
- Trinity can complete in subagent contexts or at least behaves materially better there.
- It does **not** catastrophically wedge the entire user-facing session surface the way main session does.

### Main session lane
- Switching main session to `arcee/trinity-large-thinking` leads to repeated connection errors.
- TUI spams `connection error`.
- Telegram becomes nonresponsive.
- Fallback eventually occurs, but too late to preserve UX.

## Fresh evidence from main session failures
From `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log`:

```text
2026-04-07T22:17:10.473-04:00 [agent] embedded run agent end: runId=7b00bf0c-c380-43a3-ab31-93cef91d2346 isError=true model=trinity-large-thinking provider=arcee error=LLM request failed: network connection error. rawError=Connection error.
2026-04-07T22:17:13.901-04:00 [agent] embedded run agent end: runId=7b00bf0c-c380-43a3-ab31-93cef91d2346 isError=true model=trinity-large-thinking provider=arcee error=LLM request failed: network connection error. rawError=Connection error.
2026-04-07T22:17:19.323-04:00 [agent] embedded run agent end: runId=7b00bf0c-c380-43a3-ab31-93cef91d2346 isError=true model=trinity-la

items.title: feat: one-command EC2 service deployment with deploy.sh + ec2-setup.sh
items.content:
## Summary

  - Add `ec2-setup.sh` — runs on EC2 via SSM to build the admin console frontend, install gateway services, and start systemd units
  (`openclaw-admin`, `tenant-router`, `bedrock-proxy-h2`)
  - Extend `deploy.sh` step 8 to package services to S3 and invoke `ec2-setup.sh` on EC2, with `--skip-services` flag for infra-only
  re-deploys
  - Write `/etc/openclaw/env` via S3 upload instead of heredoc-in-SSM (the old approach silently failed, leaving the env file empty on the
  instance)
  - Fix `DYNAMODB_REGION` default from hardcoded `us-east-2` to `$REGION` in `deploy.sh`
  - Fix `AWS_REGION` default from `us-east-2` to `us-east-1` in `admin-console/start.sh`
  - Fix `FileSystemId` casing typo in CloudFormation ECS task definition
  - Fix Docker build context path and auth-agent packaging
  - Add pip3 bootstrap fallback for seed scripts

  ## Motivation

  Previously, deploying the enterprise services (admin console, tenant router, bedrock proxy) on EC2 required manual steps after
  CloudFormation completed. This PR makes `bash deploy.sh` a true one-command deploy that handles everything end-to-end.

  The region bugs were discovered when the admin console kept failing with `DynamoDB ResourceNotFoundException` — the env file was never
  written to EC2, so all services fell back to wrong hardcoded defaults.

  ## Files changed

  | File | Change |
  |------|--------|
  | `enterprise/ec2-setup.sh` | **New** — EC2 on-instance setup script |
  | `enterprise/deploy.sh` | Steps 7-8: SSM secrets, env file via S3, service deployment |
  | `enterprise/admin-console/start.sh` | Fix default AWS_REGION |
  | `enterprise/clawdbot-bedrock-agentcore-multitenancy.yaml` | Fix EFS typo |

  ## Test plan

  - [ ] Run `bash deploy.sh` on a fresh stack — verify all 3 services start and admin console login works
  - [ ] Run `bash deploy.sh --skip-services` — verify it skips step 8
  - [ ] Verify `/etc/openclaw/env` on EC2 contains correct `DYNA

items.title: feat: Minerva CLI v2 — domain-specific investment research tools
items.content:
## What this PR does

Implements the Minerva CLI v2 — a stripped-down, domain-specific investment research CLI designed to work alongside OpenClaw.

### New commands
- `minerva extract` / `extract-many` — LLM-powered information extraction (Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite)
- `minerva sec bulk-download` — download full filing library (10-Ks, 10-Qs, earnings, financials)
- `minerva sec download` — save individual filing (HTML or markdown)
- `minerva fileinfo` — file metadata + routing recommendations
- `minerva research` — deep web research (Parallel.ai, pending API access)
- `minerva analyze ngrams` / `topics` — data-driven text analysis
- `minerva plot wordcloud` — word cloud from text with financial stopwords
- `minerva valuation report` — comprehensive multi-method valuation report

### Improved commands
- All valuation commands: `--fcf` now optional, `--export` flag, full methodology in help
- SEC: fixed 13F form code + column names, all commands have EDGAR identity auto-setup
- Plot: Minerva Classic + Bloomberg themes, watermark, financial axis formatters
- All errors follow structured format (What went wrong / What to do / Alternatives)
- All commands show full help when called with missing args

### Removed (replaced by OpenClaw native tools)
- File I/O (cat/ls/write/stat/rm/grep), web search/fetch, memory, knowledge, report, codex, agentic loop, delegate reads

### Performance
- Startup: 2.4s → 0.5s via lazy imports
- SDK: switched from deprecated google-generativeai to google-genai

### Tests
40/40 passing. Live-tested against EDGAR (AAPL, Berkshire 13F) and Gemini API.

**Spec:** `docs/02-investment-harness.md`

items.title: [Bug] Main lane failover is too slow for provider transport failures, allowing TUI/Telegram degradation before fallback
items.content:
## Summary
When `arcee/trinity-large-thinking` fails in the **main session lane**, OpenClaw appears to keep retrying long enough to visibly spam `connection error` in the TUI and make Telegram nonresponsive before fallback kicks in.

This looks like a lane-isolation / fail-fast problem, not merely a provider outage.

## Why this deserves its own issue
Even if the provider is flaky, the main lane should not become user-visible sludge while waiting for fallback. The fallback exists, but it is firing too late to protect the UX.

## Evidence
Fresh same-session logs:

```text
2026-04-07T22:17:10.473-04:00 [agent] embedded run agent end: runId=7b00bf0c-c380-43a3-ab31-93cef91d2346 isError=true model=trinity-large-thinking provider=arcee error=LLM request failed: network connection error. rawError=Connection error.
2026-04-07T22:17:13.901-04:00 [agent] embedded run agent end: runId=7b00bf0c-c380-43a3-ab31-93cef91d2346 isError=true model=trinity-large-thinking provider=arcee error=LLM request failed: network connection error. rawError=Connection error.
2026-04-07T22:17:19.323-04:00 [agent] embedded run agent end: runId=7b00bf0c-c380-43a3-ab31-93cef91d2346 isError=true model=trinity-large-thinking provider=arcee error=LLM request failed: network connection error. rawError=Connection error.
2026-04-07T22:17:28.686-04:00 [agent] embedded run agent end: runId=7b00bf0c-c380-43a3-ab31-93cef91d2346 isError=true model=trinity-large-thinking provider=arcee error=LLM request failed: network connection error. rawError=Connection error.
2026-04-07T22:17:29.704-04:00 [agent] embedded run failover decision: runId=7b00bf0c-c380-43a3-ab31-93cef91d2346 stage=assistant decision=fallback_model reason=timeout provider=arcee/trinity-large-thinking profile=-
2026-04-07T22:17:29.705-04:00 [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=main durationMs=23552 error="FailoverError: LLM request failed: network connection error."

items.title: [Bug] Main session can brick on arcee/trinity-large-thinking: repeated connection errors, TUI spam, Telegram nonresponsive until delayed fallback
items.content:
## Summary
Switching the **main session** model to `arcee/trinity-large-thinking` can brick the session: the TUI repeatedly shows `connection error`, Telegram becomes nonresponsive, and the main lane stalls until fallback finally fires ~20-24s later. The same model can work in other contexts, which makes this a dangerous weak link in the main-session path.

This is not fixed by uncapping output tokens in config and restarting.

## Severity
High. This degrades the primary interactive session path and effectively wedges both the local TUI and Telegram responsiveness whenever Trinity is selected as the main session model.

## Environment
- OpenClaw runtime: main agent session on macOS
- Model: `arcee/trinity-large-thinking` (alias `trinity`)
- Provider: `arcee`
- Session type affected: **main session**
- Observed date: 2026-04-07

## What we already ruled out
We explicitly tested the obvious config variable and ruled it out:
- Trinity config persists correctly
- output cap was uncapped in config
- gateway restarted cleanly
- failure still persists

So this is **not just a token-cap config issue**.

## User-visible behavior
1. Switch main session to Trinity
2. TUI starts spamming `connection error`
3. Telegram goes dark / nonresponsive
4. Session appears bricked until fallback eventually succeeds on another model

This is not a graceful provider failure. It is a main-session stability failure.

## Fresh log evidence (last ~15 min after uncap + restart)
From `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log`:

```text
2026-04-07T22:15:40.639-04:00 [agent] embedded run agent end: runId=c40546af-1962-4622-ac32-cff3b3006ba9 isError=true model=trinity-large-thinking provider=arcee error=LLM request failed: network connection error. rawError=Connection error.
2026-04-07T22:15:44.125-04:00 [agent] embedded run agent end: runId=c40546af-1962-4622-ac32-cff3b3006ba9 isError=true model=trinity-large-thinking provider=arcee error=LLM request failed: network connection error. rawError=Connection er

items.title: Upstream release: openclaw v2026.4.7
items.content:
## Release Notes (first 100 lines)

Upstream version: `v2026.4.7`
Release URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.7
Previous stable version: `v2026.4.5`
Release diff: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/compare/v2026.4.5...v2026.4.7

### Changelog excerpt
```
### Changes
- CLI/infer: add a first-class `openclaw infer ...` hub for provider-backed inference workflows across model, media, web, and embedding tasks. Thanks @Takhoffman.
- Tools/media generation: auto-fallback across auth-backed image, music, and video providers by default, preserve intent during provider switches, remap size/aspect/resolution/duration hints to the closest supported option, and surface provider capabilities plus mode-aware video-to-video support.
- Memory/wiki: restore the bundled `memory-wiki` stack with plugin, CLI, sync/query/apply tooling, memory-host integration, structured claim/evidence fields, compiled digest retrieval, claim-health linting, contradiction clustering, staleness dashboards, and freshness-weighted search. Thanks @vincentkoc.
- Plugins/webhooks: add a bundled webhook ingress plugin so external automation can create and drive bound TaskFlows through per-route shared-secret endpoints. (#61892) Thanks @mbelinky.
- Gateway/sessions: add persisted compaction checkpoints plus Sessions UI branch/restore actions so operators can inspect and recover pre-compaction session state. (#62146) Thanks @scoootscooob.
- Compaction: add pluggable compaction provider registry so plugins can replace the built-in summarization pipeline. Configure via `agents.defaults.compaction.provider`; falls back to LLM summarization on provider failure. (#56224) Thanks @DhruvBhatia0.
- Agents/system prompt: add `agents.defaults.systemPromptOverride` for controlled prompt experiments plus heartbeat prompt-section controls so heartbeat runtime behavior can stay enabled without injecting heartbeat instructions every turn.
- Providers/Google: add Gemma 4 model support and keep Google

items.title: feat: MCP agent integration -- rich descriptions, webhooks, negotiation tools, API key management
items.content:
## MCP Agent Integration

Makes the existing MCP server reliable enough for external AI agents (Hermes, OpenClaw) to autonomously use Index for discovery and negotiations.

### New Features

- **Rich tool descriptions** -- all 28+ tool descriptions rewritten with domain context, parameter guidance, and workflow info for MCP consumers
- **Enhanced `read_docs` tool** -- comprehensive domain guide covering core concepts, workflows, negotiation model, and auth
- **Webhook infrastructure (IND-223)** -- domain-agnostic push notification system with HMAC-SHA256 signing, BullMQ delivery, auto-disable after failures
  - 6 events: opportunity.created/accepted/rejected, negotiation.started/turn_received/completed
  - 5 MCP tools: register_webhook, list_webhooks, delete_webhook, test_webhook, list_webhook_events
  - REST API: `/api/webhooks` CRUD endpoints
- **Negotiation MCP tools** -- `list_negotiations`, `get_negotiation`, `respond_to_negotiation`
  - Symmetric design: either/both parties can be external agents
  - Per-turn resolution: webhook check happens fresh each turn
- **Negotiation graph yield/resume** -- graph pauses when external agent has webhook, resumes on tool call or AI fallback after 24h timeout
- **API key management UI** -- settings page with create/list/delete, MCP config snippets for Hermes/OpenClaw/Claude Code

### Documentation

- Design spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-08-mcp-agent-integration-design.md`
- Implementation plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-08-mcp-agent-integration-plan.md`

### Database

- New `webhooks` table (migration `0036_add_webhooks_table.sql`)

### Closes

- IND-223 (Event webhooks protocol implementation)

  * Webhooks: register/list/delete/test webhooks, signed deliveries with retries, auto-disable after failures, background delivery and timeout workers.

items.content:
## Summary

- Restructured k8s-ops-agent from a monolithic OpenClaw plugin into a pnpm monorepo with 4 packages
- **@k8s-ops/core**: Platform-agnostic SDK with 32 skill handlers, skill registry, and shared utilities
- **@k8s-ops/mcp**: MCP Server (stdio transport) for Claude Code, Cursor, and other AI tools
- **@k8s-ops/cli**: CLI toolkit with 32 auto-generated commands via commander
- **@k8s-ops/openclaw**: Thin OpenClaw adapter consuming core skillRegistry (~40 lines vs original ~130)
- Fixed multiple TypeScript strict mode issues (zod v4 z.record, k8s client-node type compatibility)
- 420 tests passing across 3 packages (core: 413, mcp: 4, cli: 3)

## Architecture

```
packages/
├── core/          # 32 skill handlers + registry + lib (684K)
├── mcp-server/    # MCP Server adapter (16K)
├── cli/           # CLI adapter (24K)
└── openclaw-plugin/ # OpenClaw adapter (8K)
```

All adapters consume `skillRegistry` from `@k8s-ops/core` — zero manual wiring for tool registration.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm build` — all 4 packages compile successfully
- [x] `pnpm test` — 420 tests pass (32 test files)
- [x] MCP Server smoke test — responds to `initialize` with tools capability
- [x] CLI smoke test — `--help` shows all 32 commands
- [x] Old monolithic files (`skills/`, `lib/`, `index.ts`) removed after migration
- [ ] Manual verification: install OpenClaw plugin from new path
- [ ] Manual verification: run MCP server with a real K8s cluster

## OpenClaw 项目深度报告

# OpenClaw 项目动态日报（2026-04-08）

---

## 1. 今日速览

OpenClaw 社区在24小时内保持高度活跃，共产生 **500条 Issues 更新**（新开/活跃 437，已关闭 63）和 **500条 PR 更新**（待合并 370，已合并/关闭 130），显示出强劲的开发与问题反馈节奏。尽管无新版本发布，但多个关键回归 Bug 被识别并已有修复 PR 提交，表明团队正集中应对近期版本（如 v2026.4.x）引入的稳定性问题。Windows 平台兼容性、插件加载机制、LLM 超时控制及多通道消息同步是当前主要痛点。

---

## 2. 版本发布

**无新版本发布**。最近一次稳定版本仍为 `v2026.4.5`，但该版本暴露出多个回归问题（见下文 Bug 部分），社区正积极修复中。

---

## 3. 项目进展

今日合并/关闭的重要 PR 聚焦于 **稳定性修复与核心功能增强**：

- **#62794**：修复浏览器端 AI 快照引用格式错误，确保 action resolver 对齐（[链接](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/62794)）
- **#62797**：优化 Cron 任务认证配置，解决隔离作业中 auth profile 不稳定问题（[链接](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/62797)）
- **#62789**：新增 CLI 对 command 类型 cron job 的支持，提升本地自动化能力（[链接](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/62789)）
- **#62788**：为 WhatsApp  inbound voice notes 添加预转录支持，补齐与 Telegram/Discord 功能一致性（[链接](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/62788)）
- **#62775**：默认启用循环检测机制，防止小模型陷入无限工具调用循环（关键安全修复）（[链接](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/62775)）

> 整体来看，项目正从“功能扩张”转向“稳定性加固”，尤其在多代理运行时、插件兼容性与跨平台支持方面取得实质性进展。

---

## 4. 社区热点

以下 Issues 引发高度关注，反映用户核心诉求：

| Issue | 热度 | 核心诉求 |
|------|------|--------|
| [#75 Linux/Windows Clawdbot Apps](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/75) | 👍68, 评论76 | 强烈呼吁补齐 Linux/Windows 原

items.title: Task ideas from canitrunopenclaw/ClawBench analysis
items.content:
## Overview

Analyzed [canitrunopenclaw](https://github.com/codyrobertson/canitrunopenclaw) and its ClawBench benchmarking tool.

**What it is:** A hardware compatibility directory for OpenClaw forks. ClawBench measures *installation and startup performance*, not agent task completion.

**Key difference from PinchBench:**
- **ClawBench** = Can the fork *run* on this hardware? (clone time, install time, disk usage, memory, startup)
- **PinchBench** = Can the agent *complete tasks* correctly? (task accuracy, tool usage, reasoning)

## ClawBench Scoring (for reference)

| Component | Weight | What it measures |
|-----------|--------|------------------|
| Latency | 30 pts | Cold start time (clone + install + startup) |
| Capabilities | 40 pts | 8 capability checks (messaging, browser, code exec, memory, files, search, MCP, tool use) |
| Size | 20 pts | Total disk footprint after install |
| Build | 10 pts | Successful install + successful startup |

**Capabilities detected via static analysis:**
- Messaging (WhatsApp/Telegram/Discord/Slack)
- Browser automation (Puppeteer/Playwright/Selenium)
- Code execution (subprocess/child_process)
- Persistent memory (SQLite/Redis/vectordb)
- File management
- Web search
- MCP support
- Tool use

## Relevant Ideas for PinchBench

While ClawBench doesn't have agent *tasks* to port, some capability checks could inspire new task categories:

### 1. MCP Server Integration Task
ClawBench checks for MCP support. We could add a task where the agent must:
- Connect to an MCP server
- Discover available tools
- Use an MCP-provided tool to complete a task

**Why:** MCP is increasingly important in the OpenClaw ecosystem.

### 2. Multi-Channel Messaging Task
ClawBench checks for messaging platform support. Task idea:
- Send a message via one channel (e.g., mock Discord webhook)
- Verify delivery or response

**Challenge:** Requires mock infrastructure or webhook.

### 3. Browser + Code Execution Combined Task
ClawBench separately checks brows

items.title: [Bug]: ACP client hangs after prompt and shows tick timeout / invalid handshake
items.content:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

OpenClaw ACP client creates a session successfully but hangs after a prompt, with observed tick timeout and invalid handshake errors in logs.


### Steps to reproduce

1. Run `openclaw acp client --verbose` on Windows with OpenClaw 2026.4.5.
2. Wait for the ACP client session to be created successfully.
3. Enter a simple prompt such as `hello`.
4. Wait about 30 seconds.
5. Observe that no response is returned and the client hangs or later logs timeout/handshake errors.


### Expected behavior

After entering a simple prompt such as `hello`, the ACP client should return a normal assistant response instead of hanging.


### Actual behavior

The ACP client creates a session successfully, but after entering a prompt it hangs without producing a response.

Observed logs include:
- `Gateway disconnected: 4000: tick timeout`
- `gateway closed (1008): invalid handshake: first request must be connect`
- gateway/ws logs showing `lastFrameMethod":"agent.wait"` and `handshakeError":"invalid handshake: first request must be connect"`


### OpenClaw version

2026.4.5 (3e72c03)

### Operating system

Windows 10.0.26200

### Install method

npm global

### Model

openai-codex/gpt-5.4

### Provider / routing chain

openclaw -> local gateway -> openai-codex/gpt-5.4

### Additional provider/model setup details

Observed config includes:
- `plugins.entries.acpx.enabled = true`
- `plugins.entries.acpx.config.pluginToolsMcpBridge = true`

During earlier debugging, `plugins.entries.anthropic.enabled` was set to `false` because logs showed:
- `anthropic failed to load ... RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded`
- `Failed to read config ... RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded`

After disabling anthropic, plugin errors no longer appeared in doctor output, but the ACP client hang remained.

Observed logs:

- `Error handling request ... d
