items.title: Blog: Navigating the AI Memory and Code Intelligence Landscape — voitta-rag, Graphify, and MemPalace
items.content:
# Navigating the AI Memory and Code Intelligence Landscape: voitta-rag, Graphify, and MemPalace

*An independent look at three tools trying to solve the same fundamental problem: how do you give an AI assistant the right context at the right time?*

---

## The Problem Space

Every developer who's used an AI coding assistant has hit the same wall. The model is brilliant in isolation, but it doesn't *know your codebase*. It doesn't remember what you told it yesterday. It can't trace a call graph or tell you why that authentication middleware exists. The current generation of tools attacking this problem falls into three broad categories: **retrieval** (search your code), **structure** (understand your code), and **memory** (remember your conversations). voitta-rag, Graphify, and MemPalace each stake out different territory -- and the interesting question is how they overlap, conflict, and complement each other.

---

## voitta-rag: The Retrieval Workhorse

**What it is:** A self-hosted RAG server that indexes codebases (and increasingly, enterprise data sources) into Qdrant vector chunks and exposes them via MCP tools. Agents search, get real source code back, and can progressively expand context via chunk ranges.

### Strengths

- **Actually returns your code.** Unlike tools that return abstractions or summaries, voitta-rag gives you the implementation. When an agent needs to modify a function, it gets the function -- not a description of it.
- **Hybrid search with tunable precision.** The `sparse_weight` parameter lets you dial between pure semantic and keyword matching. This matters more than it sounds -- semantic search alone hallucinates relevance; keyword search alone misses conceptual matches.
- **Enterprise connector breadth.** Git, Jira, Confluence, SharePoint, Google Drive, Azure DevOps, Box, AWS Glue Data Catalog. This isn't a toy for solo developers; it's aimed at teams with scattered knowledge.
- **Per-folder visibility toggles.** The March 2026 release
items.author: debedb

items.title: feat: bypass 10-person limit via Node.js interceptor + keyword-driven contact search
items.content:
`get_userlist` hard-limits responses to 10 members, making it unusable in large orgs. The fix intercepts the call at the Node.js layer to fetch the full list silently, filter by keyword in-memory, and return only the matched subset — while requiring LLM prompts to always supply a `keyword` instead of dumping the entire directory into context.

## Changes

### Node.js interceptor (`src/mcp/interceptors/`)
- **New** `contact-search.ts`: Implements `CallInterceptor` for `contact/get_userlist`
  - `beforeCall`: strips `keyword` from args before forwarding upstream (prevents server-side validation error), forcing a full-list fetch
  - `afterCall`: filters returned `userlist` by `keyword` against `name`/`alias` (case-insensitive), caps at 10 matches
  - Typed via `GetUserlistResult` interface — no `any` casts
- **`index.ts`**: registers `contactSearchInterceptor` in the pipeline

```typescript
// LLM sends:  get_userlist '{"keyword": "张三"}'
// Interceptor forwards: get_userlist '{}'  →  full list from server
// Interceptor returns to LLM: userlist filtered to ≤10 matches for "张三"
```

### Skill prompts (`skills/`)
- **`wecom-contact-lookup/SKILL.md`**: Replaced full-dump approach with mandatory `keyword` param; `{}` explicitly forbidden
- **`wecom-meeting-create/SKILL.md`**: Workflow now issues one `get_userlist` call per invitee with their name as keyword
- **`wecom-edit-todo/SKILL.md`**: Contact lookup step uses keyword search
- **`wecom-get-todo-detail/SKILL.md`**: Removed all `get_userlist '{}'` calls; clarified that keywords must be names (not internal IDs, which the interceptor cannot match)
items.author: Copilot

items.title: fix: inject conductor MCP server into Claude Code and QwenCode dispatchers
items.content:
## Type of Change
- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] Feature (new functionality)

## Summary

The dispatcher was completely broken for Claude Code and QwenCode agents. Only Codex received the conductor MCP server config, meaning only Codex could call \`conductor_get_board\`, \`conductor_dispatcher_create_task\`, \`conductor_dispatcher_update_task\`, and \`conductor_dispatcher_handoff_task\`.

Without these MCP tools, the dispatcher agent (regardless of which coding agent is used) could not:
- Read the current board state
- Create tasks on the board
- Update existing tasks
- Hand off tasks to implementation agents

This is why "the dispatcher chat is not doing anything" for non-Codex agents.

## Changes

### \`crates/conductor-server/src/state/mcp_config.rs\`
- Added \`build_claude_mcp_config_json()\`: generates a Claude Code compatible \`--mcp-config\` JSON string from the merged MCP server map. Claude Code expects \`--mcp-config <json>\` where json is \`{ "mcpServers": { ... } }\`.
- Added 2 unit tests for the new function

### \`crates/conductor-server/src/state/mod.rs\`
- Added \`claude_mcp_extra_args()\`: mirrors \`codex_mcp_extra_args()\` but outputs \`--mcp-config <json>\` for Claude Code's CLI format instead of Codex's \`-c\` TOML overrides

### \`crates/conductor-server/src/state/acp_dispatcher.rs\`
- Changed \`ensure_dispatcher_runtime()\` to use a match on \`AgentKind\`:
  - **Codex / QwenCode**: use \`codex_mcp_extra_args\` (existing \`-c\` config format)
  - **ClaudeCode**: use \`claude_mcp_extra_args\` (new \`--mcp-config\` JSON format)
  - **OpenClaw**: no MCP injection (uses WebSocket protocol, not CLI)

## OpenClaw Limitation

OpenClaw dispatchers still cannot use conductor MCP tools because the OpenClaw executor communicates via WebSocket to the gateway, not via CLI MCP. Users who want OpenClaw dispatchers should configure the conductor MCP server in their OpenClaw gateway config (\`openclaw.json\`).

items.title: OAuth authentication: PKCE flow, token refresh with mutex, client factory
items.content:
## Summary

Implement Nova's OAuth authentication system that makes requests indistinguishable from Claude Code. This is the identity layer — every API call depends on it.

## Architecture

Nova authenticates as Claude Code using OAuth 2.0 with PKCE. The system has two modes:
1. **Bootstrap (local)**: Browser-based OAuth flow to get initial refresh token
2. **Runtime (VPS)**: Headless token refresh using stored refresh token

### OAuth Identity (MUST match Claude Code exactly)
- **Client ID**: `9d1c250a-e61b-44d9-88ed-5944d1962f5e`
- **Scopes**: `user:profile user:inference user:sessions:claude_code user:mcp_servers user:file_upload`
- **Token URL**: `https://platform.claude.com/v1/oauth/token`
- **API Endpoint**: `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages`

## Files to Implement

### `src/auth/oauth.ts` — OAuth PKCE Flow
- Generate PKCE code verifier (43-128 char random string) and challenge (SHA256 + base64url)
- Build authorization URL with all required params
- Start local HTTP server on `localhost:PORT` to receive callback
- Exchange authorization code for tokens via POST to token URL
- Store refresh token securely

### `src/auth/tokens.ts` — Token Storage & Refresh
- **Token storage**: SQLite `oauth_tokens` table (id, access_token, refresh_token, expires_at, scopes, updated_at)
- **Proactive auto-refresh**: Set a timer to refresh 5 minutes before token expiry (not on-demand)
- **Mutex on refresh**: Promise-based lock to prevent race conditions when multiple agents detect expiry simultaneously:
  ```typescript
  let refreshPromise: Promise<string> | null = null;
  async function getAccessToken(): Promise<string> {
    if (isTokenValid()) return currentToken;
    if (refreshPromise) return refreshPromise; // another caller is already refreshing
    refreshPromise = doRefresh().finally(() => { refreshPromise = null; });
    return refreshPromise;
  }
  ```
- **Token invalidation handling**: If refresh fails with auth error (401/403), alert owner via Telegram/Discord:

# OpenClaw Project Digest — 2026-04-08

---

# ArXiv AI 研究日报 | 2026-04-08

---

## 今日速览

今日ArXiv共收录50篇AI核心论文，**推理模型优化与智能体安全**成为两大焦点。长思维链（CoT）的内存瓶颈与计算效率问题催生多项创新，包括三角函数KV压缩与动态早停机制；同时，AI智能体的实际部署风险引发高度关注，OpenClaw安全分析与不可检测代理通信等研究揭示了前沿能力与安全隐患的并行演进。此外，视觉推理的开放配方、数学证明的小型化等方向展现了能力 democratization 的趋势。

---

## 重点论文

### 🧠 大语言模型

| 论文 | 作者 | 一句话说明 |
|:---|:---|:---|
| [**TriAttention: Efficient Long Reasoning with Trigonometric KV Compression**](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.04921v1) | Mao et al. | 利用三角函数性质压缩KV缓存，解决长推理链的内存瓶颈，突破RoPE旋转查询导致的代表性不足问题。 |
| [**Early Stopping for Large Reasoning Models via Confidence Dynamics**](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.04930v1) | Hosseini et al. | 通过监测推理过程中的置信度动态变化实现早停，降低"过度思考"导致的计算浪费与性能退化。 |
| [**Are Latent Reasoning Models Easily Interpretable?**](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.04902v1) | Dilgren & Wiegreffe | 系统分析隐式推理模型（LRMs）的可解释性局限，揭示并行推理路径探索与透明度之间的根本张力。 |
| [**Cog-DRIFT: Exploration on Adaptively Reformulated Instances Enables Learning from Hard Reasoning Problems**](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.04767v1) | Chen et al. | 通过自适应问题重构让模型从当前无法解决的难题中学习，突破RLVR的奖励稀疏性瓶颈。 |
| [**Rethinking Exploration in RLVR: From Entropy Regularization to Refinement via Bidirectional Entropy Modulation**](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.04894v1) | Gu et al. | 提出双向熵调制机制，解决RLVR中策略快速收敛至狭窄解空间的"受限探索"问题。 |
| [**QED-Nano: Teaching a Tiny Model to Prove Hard Theorems**](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.04898v1) | LM-Provers et al. | 开源小型模型（7B）达到IMO金牌水平，揭示专有系统的训练秘密，推动数学推理能力民主化。 |

    *   **描述:** 此 PR 旨在解决 Matrix 通道启动时报告成功但同步未就绪，以及监控任务在无所有者时可能拒绝的问题。通过防止单个通道故障升级为进程范围的崩溃循环，显著增强了 Matrix 通道的稳定性。

items.title: [Feature]: Support OAuth-based remote MCP authentication flows (Heptabase MCP)
items.content:
### Summary

Support OAuth-based authentication for remote MCP servers such as Heptabase MCP.


### Problem to solve

I can register a remote MCP server in OpenClaw using its URL, and OpenClaw does attempt to connect to it. However, some MCP servers such as Heptabase require OAuth authentication rather than a static bearer token.

In this case, OpenClaw reaches the MCP endpoint but fails with 401 Unauthorized because the OAuth authorization flow does not complete.

Current behavior makes it difficult or impossible to use OAuth-based remote MCP servers even when the endpoint is valid and OpenClaw can reach it.


### Proposed solution

Add a supported OAuth flow for remote MCP servers.

For example:
- allow OpenClaw to detect that a remote MCP server requires OAuth
- provide a CLI or Control UI flow to start authentication
- open the authorization page in the browser
- complete the redirect/callback flow
- persist the authenticated connection for future MCP sessions

This should work for remote MCP services like Heptabase MCP, where the documented flow is:
1. add MCP server URL
2. authenticate via OAuth in browser
3. return to the client with the connection established


### Alternatives considered

A manual workaround using static headers is not sufficient here because Heptabase MCP appears to use OAuth rather than a manually copied bearer token.

Another workaround is manually copying data from Heptabase into OpenClaw memory, but that defeats the goal of using MCP as a live integration.


### Impact

Affected: users trying to connect OAuth-based remote MCP servers such as Heptabase
Severity: blocks intended workflow
Frequency: always for OAuth-only MCP servers
Consequence: remote MCP integration appears reachable but cannot be completed, forcing manual copy/paste workflows instead of live knowledge integration


### Evidence/examples

Observed behavior in logs:

- failed to start server "heptabase" (https://api.heptabase.com/mcp): Error: SSE error: Non-200 status co

items.author: syzsunshine219

items.title: [Bug] Docker HEALTHCHECK uses node -e fetch which fails intermittently in 4.5+
items.content:
## Bug Description

Docker health check in the official image consistently fails (container marked as unhealthy) even when the Gateway is running correctly and /healthz returns HTTP 200.

## Environment

- OpenClaw version: 4.5 (and later)
- Docker base image: node:24-bookworm
- Deployment: Docker container with port mapping (-p 4000:18789)

## Reproduction

1. Build or pull openclaw 4.5+ image
2. Run container: `docker run -d --name openclaw -p 4000:18789 ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:latest openclaw gateway run`
3. Observe: `docker ps` shows container as **unhealthy**
4. Inside container, `curl http://127.0.0.1:18789/healthz` returns **HTTP 200 OK** — gateway is fine

## Root Cause

The Dockerfile contains:

```dockerfile
HEALTHCHECK --interval=3m --timeout=10s --start-period=15s --retries=3 \
  CMD node -e "fetch('http://127.0.0.1:18789/healthz').then((r)=>process.exit(r.ok?0:1)).catch(()=>process.exit(1))"
```

Issues:
1. **Node fetch is asynchronous** — the `node -e fetch(...)` command exits immediately after the promise is created, before the network request completes, causing `exec_die` events
2. **Node 24 fetch behavior** — in some container startup scenarios, the global fetch API may not be properly initialized in the short window before the health check fires
3. **start-period=15s is insufficient** — when the gateway is still initializing plugins and channels, the health check fires and fails

In contrast, `curl -f http://127.0.0.1:18789/healthz` works correctly and returns 200.

## Expected Fix

Replace the HEALTHCHECK command with a curl-based check:

```dockerfile
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=30s --retries=3 \
  CMD curl -f http://127.0.0.1:18789/healthz || exit 1
```

`curl` is:
- Already installed in the image (confirmed in Dockerfile: `apt-get install ... curl`)
- More reliable as a static system binary
- The standard approach used by most production Docker images
- Not affected by Node.js runtime initialization timing

## Addi

items.title: fix(microsoft,elevenlabs): add enabledByDefault so speech providers register at runtime
items.content:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Microsoft TTS provider is not available at runtime — `/tts provider` only shows OpenAI, and `/tts status` reports `microsoft (not configured)` with `skipped(no_provider_registered)`. ElevenLabs has the same structural issue.
- **Why it matters:** Users who configure Microsoft TTS voices (e.g. `zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`) get no TTS output despite correct config. The gateway silently falls back to OpenAI.
- **What changed:** Added `"enabledByDefault": true` to `extensions/microsoft/openclaw.plugin.json` and `extensions/elevenlabs/openclaw.plugin.json`, matching the pattern of all other working bundled speech provider plugins (openai, minimax, vydra).
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No code changes. The provider registration code in `index.ts` and `speech-provider.ts` for both plugins is correct — they register properly once the plugin is actually loaded. The gap was purely in the activation gate.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

- [ ] Refactor required for the fix

- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #62574
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** `extensions/microsoft/openclaw.plugin.json` and `extensions/elevenlabs/openclaw.plugin.json` are missing `"enabledByDefault": true`. At gateway startup, `resolveEffectivePluginActivationState()` in `src/plugins/config-policy.ts` (line 172) checks `params.enabledByDefault === true` for bundled plugins. Without this field, the plugins return `{ enabled: false, reason: "bundled (disabled by default)" }` and are never loaded into the active runtime registry.

items.title: [Bug]:  Agent listener invoked outside active run - Gateway crashes on WhatsApp message
items.content:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

Gateway crashes with unhandled promise rejection when a WhatsApp message arrives while no active agent run exists.
### Error
Error: Agent listener invoked outside active run
    at Agent.processEvents (/home/fsociety/.bun/install/global/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-agent-core/dist/agent.js:388)
    at file:///.../pi-agent-core/src/agent-loop.ts:539
### Steps to reproduce
1. Agent finishes processing a message (run ends)
2. Something runs in background (audio/music task)
3. New WhatsApp message arrives
4. Gateway crashes with exit-code 1
### Fix suggestion (AI-assisted via OpenCode)
In `@mariozechner/pi-agent-core/dist/agent.js`, line ~388:
**Before:**
```javascript
if (!signal) {
    throw new Error("Agent listener invoked outside active run");
}
After:
if (!signal) {
    console.warn("[pi-agent] Event received outside active run, skipping listeners");
    return;
}
This prevents the crash by gracefully skipping events when no active run exists, instead of throwing an unhandled rejection.
Status: ✅ RESOLVED
We were able to fix this bug ourselves using AI assistance (OpenCode). The patch was applied and the gateway is now stable.
Environment
- OpenClaw: 2026.4.5
- Node: 22.22.1
- OS: Kali Linux
- AI Tool: OpenCode (opencode.ai) with Claude
Suggested workaround
See fix above, or wait for official upstream patch.

### Steps to reproduce

Gateway crashes with unhandled promise rejection when a WhatsApp message arrives while no active agent run exists.
### Error
Error: Agent listener invoked outside active run
    at Agent.processEvents (/home/fsociety/.bun/install/global/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-agent-core/dist/agent.js:388)
    at file:///.../pi-agent-core/src/agent-loop.ts:539
### Steps to reproduce
1. Agent finishes processing a message (run ends)
2. Something runs in background (audio/music task)
3. New WhatsApp message arrives
4. Gateway crashes with exit-code 1

items.title: Webchat: ephemeral prependContext from plugins leaks into user-visible messages
items.content:
## Description

When a plugin injects content via `before_prompt_build` with `prependContext` and `ephemeral: true`, the injected content should only be visible to the agent (as system prompt context) and never displayed to the user in webchat.

## Observed Behavior

With `memory-lancedb-pro` plugin installed, the auto-recall feature injects memory blocks wrapped in `<relevant-memories>` tags with `ephemeral: true`. These memory blocks occasionally appear as visible messages in the webchat UI, showing content like:

```
<relevant-memories>
<mode:summary>
[UNTRUSTED DATA — historical notes from long-term memory...]
- [fact:default] ...
</relevant-memories>
```

## Expected Behavior

The `ephemeral: true` flag should cause the webchat frontend to completely filter out the injected content from the user-facing message stream.

## Environment

- OpenClaw: 2026.4.2 (d74a122)
- Plugin: memory-lancedb-pro v1.1.0-beta.10
- Channel: webchat
- OS: Linux 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2 (x64)

## Notes

- The plugin code correctly sets `ephemeral: true` in the `before_prompt_build` return value
- The issue is on the webchat frontend side not respecting this flag

items.title: Cron isolated session exec tool result not delivered to parent model after upgrade to 2026.4.5
items.content:
## Bug Description

After upgrading from 2026.3.28 to 2026.4.5, cron jobs using isolated session + agentTurn payload fail consistently with timeout errors, even though the underlying script executes successfully.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Set up a cron job with sessionTarget: \"isolated\" and agentTurn payload that runs a Python script via exec tool
2. Schedule it to run on a cron expression
3. Observe that the script executes successfully (logs confirm this) but the agent never receives the exec tool result
4. After ~60-90s the agent returns: \"Request timed out before a response was generated\"

## Expected Behavior

The agent should receive the exec tool result, generate a response, and the cron delivery mechanism should send the result to the configured channel.

## Actual Behavior

- Script execution completes successfully in the isolated session (confirmed via cron run logs showing duration ~70s for the isolated session itself)
- The parent model receives only ~111 input tokens from the exec tool result (vs. thousands when working normally)
- Model output is truncated at ~65-80 tokens with the timeout error message
- deliveryStatus shows \"delivered\" but no messageId is recorded (no Telegram message sent)

## Environment

- OpenClaw: 2026.4.5 (upgraded from 2026.3.28)
- Node.js: v24.14.0
- OS: Linux 6.8.0
- Agent: monitor (isolated session)
- Channel: telegram

## Relevant Changes in 2026.4.5

The following changes in 2026.4.5 are suspected to be related:

1. **Breaking - Config**: \"remove agents.*.sandbox.perSession, merge per-agent sandbox config into agents.*.sandbox (keyed by agent id)\" - This changes how sandbox is configured per session, which could affect isolated session behavior.

2. **ACPX/runtime**: \"embed the ACP runtime directly in the bundled acpx plugin, remove the extra external ACP CLI hop, harden live ACP session binding and reuse, and add a generic reply_dispatch hook\" - This changes session binding and reply routing, which could affec

items.author: SKY-lv

items.title: fix(dev): recover from stale web and controller listeners
items.content:
## What

Make the local `pnpm dev` flow clean up stale listeners on the fixed controller and web ports before starting those services.

## Why

The dev stack can get into a partial-stale state where the supervisor lock is gone but an old process is still listening on `50800` or `50810`. In that case `pnpm dev start` fails with `EADDRINUSE`, and desktop startup then cascades into controller readiness failures even though the issue is only a leftover local listener.

## How

- Add stale-listener cleanup in `scripts/dev/src/services/controller.ts` before starting the controller service.
- Add the same stale-listener cleanup in `scripts/dev/src/services/web.ts` before starting the web service.
- Wait briefly for each listener to disappear after termination so the start flow only proceeds once the port is actually free.

## Affected areas

- [ ] Desktop app (Electron shell)
- [ ] Controller (backend / API)
- [ ] Web dashboard (React UI)
- [ ] OpenClaw runtime
- [ ] Skills
- [ ] Shared schemas / packages
- [x] Build / CI / Tooling

## Checklist

- [x] `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [x] `pnpm lint` passes
- [ ] `pnpm test` passes
- [ ] `pnpm generate-types` run (if API routes/schemas changed)
- [x] No credentials or tokens in code or logs
- [x] No `any` types introduced (use `unknown` with narrowing)

## Summary Fixes two issues in the EAS build CI workflow: the build URL was always empty because `buildDetailsPageUrl` wasn't populated in the EAS JSON output, and Slack API errors were silently swallowed with no visibility in CI logs. **Type of change:** fix ## Key Changes - **Build URL**: Replace `buildDetailsPageUrl` (always empty) with a manually constructed URL using the build `id`: `https://expo.dev/accounts/aramiworks/projects/stock-tracker/builds/{id}` - **Slack logging**: Capture `curl` response into `$RESPONSE` and echo it to CI logs so errors like `not_in_channel` are visible - **Changeset**: Add patch changeset for INF-798 ## Testing Done - Verified diff logic matches expected EAS JSON shape (`.[0].id`) - Slack bot still needs `/invite @` in `#openclaw-hq` (`C0AJGRU7SD6`) for notifications to appear Closes INF-798 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Both fixes are straightforward and correct for the happy path, but the previously flagged issue — where an empty `BUILD_ID` produces a partial, non-empty `BUILD_URL` that silently bypasses the `${BUILD_URL:-$FALLBACK}` guard — remains unaddressed in both the `build` and `storybook` jobs.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge for the happy path, but the empty-BUILD_ID partial-URL issue flagged in prior review threads remains unresolved and will send broken Slack links on EAS JSON parse failures. Both targeted fixes (URL construction and Slack response logging) are correct. items.title: fix: unstick planning modal state and enforce poll timeouts
items.content:
## Summary

Fixes the Mission Control planning flow getting stuck on `Waiting for response...` / `Sending...` even after the backend has already advanced the task, and enforces the configured planning poll timeout through the OpenClaw transcript path.

## What changed

- make the open task modal resolve the live task by ID instead of holding a stale snapshot
- sync the modal with `selectedTask` so existing SSE updates refresh an open planning dialog
- clear sticky planning submit state when the task leaves planning, becomes approved, hits a dispatch error, or surfaces a transcript warning
- trigger an immediate refresh after answer submit instead of waiting for the next poll interval
- thread `PLANNING_TIMEOUT_MS` through `OpenClawClient.call()`, session-history loading, and planning transcript reconciliation
- return a structured planning warning on transcript timeout instead of leaving the UI spinning
- log transcript refresh / poll timing and emit a distinct `planning_poll_timeout` activity event

## Verification

- authenticated local health check returned `200 OK`
- `/workspace/squti` returned `200 OK`
- browser smoke check rendered the BoreReady workspace queue without console errors
- targeted tests passed:
  - `src/lib/planning-ui-state.test.ts`
  - `src/lib/planning-utils.test.ts`
  - `src/lib/openclaw/session-history.test.ts`
  - `src/lib/openclaw/client.test.ts`
- `npm run build` passed on Node `24.13.0`

## Notes

- this PR was cut from `origin/main` to avoid the unrelated work already present on `feat/tier-badges-on-task-cards`
- the local working tree on `feat/tier-badges-on-task-cards` still has an unrelated unstaged change in `src/lib/autopilot/ideation.ts`

items.title: fix: correct stale send_agent_message references and unify msg_type enum
items.content:
## Summary

Fixes two issues found during analysis of #310 (inter-agent communication timeouts):

### 1. Stale tool name references (`send_agent_message` → `send_message_to_agent`)

Two locations in the codebase reference `send_agent_message`, a tool name that does not exist in the tool registry. The correct name is `send_message_to_agent`.

| File | Line | Fix |
|------|------|-----|
| `backend/app/api/relationships.py` | L306 | Fixed tool name in `relationships.md` generation |
| `backend/app/api/gateway.py` | L416 | Fixed tool name in OpenClaw A2A communication prompt |

**Impact:**
- `relationships.py`: The stale name was written into the agent's `relationships.md` workspace file, which is injected into the LLM system prompt. This guided the LLM to call a non-existent tool, increasing tool call failure rate.
- `gateway.py`: The prompt told OpenClaw agents *"DO NOT use the \`send_agent_message\` tool"*. Since that tool does not exist, the LLM could attempt to call the similarly-named `send_message_to_agent` (which **does** exist) — the exact opposite of the prompt's intent, potentially causing infinite message recursion.

### 2. Inconsistent `msg_type` enum values

Unified the `msg_type` enum in `tool_seeder.py` to match `agent_tools.py`:

| Location | Before | After |
|----------|--------|-------|
| `tool_seeder.py` | `["chat", "task_request", "info_share"]` | `["notify", "consult", "task_delegate"]` |

Since `seed_builtin_tools()` upserts `parameters_schema` into the DB on every startup, and `get_agent_tools_for_llm()` reads from the DB, this change takes effect on the next application restart. The `msg_type` parameter is currently unused (dead parameter) in `_send_message_to_agent`, so this has no runtime behavioral change — it just removes the inconsistency for future development.

## Testing

Verified by creating a new agent with agent relationships after applying the fix:
- Before: `relationships.md` contained `send_agent_message` (incorrect)
- After: `rela

items.title: 【提效】构建 GLM-5.1 长时间任务 Pipeline 利用 8 小时自主能力
items.content:
## 背景

智谱发布 GLM-5.1（2026-04-08），7540 亿参数 MIT 开源，可在单次任务中自主工作长达 8 小时，通过数百轮迭代持续优化。在 SWE-Bench Pro 以 58.4 分创下全球最佳记录。

**核心价值**：GLM-5.1 的长程任务能力可承担需要数百次工具调用的复杂场景（如完整模块重构、性能优化）。

## 目标

- 探索 GLM-5.1 作为「复杂任务执行器」集成到 OpenClaw 工作流的可行性
- 设计 checkpoint + resume 机制应对长时间任务的断点续传
- 评估在 Thomas 实际项目中的适用场景

## 子任务

- [ ] 1. 申请 GLM-5.1 API（api.z.ai 或 BigModel.cn）并完成接入测试
- [ ] 2. 设计长时间任务的 checkpoint 机制（保存中间状态）
- [ ] 3. 在测试场景验证 8 小时自主工作的稳定性（断点恢复）
- [ ] 4. 对比 GLM-5.1 vs Claude Opus 4.6 在代码生成场景的质量差异
- [ ] 5. 产出「GLM-5.1 集成到 OpenClaw 工作流的落地方案」

**参考**：[GLM-5.1 发布公告](https://docs.bigmodel.cn/cn/guide/models/text/glm-5.1) | [HuggingFace](https://huggingface.co/zai-org/GLM-5.1)
items.author: ThomasLiu

items.title: fix(openai-compat): emit finish_reason stop chunk in streaming responses
items.content:
## Summary

- **Problem:** The OpenAI-compatible SSE streaming endpoint never emits a `finish_reason: "stop"` chunk in normal (non-error) completion paths. The stream goes directly from content deltas (`finish_reason: null`) to `data: [DONE]`, violating the OpenAI streaming spec.
- **Why it matters:** Client libraries (OpenAI Python/JS SDKs, LangChain, etc.) that key on `finish_reason` to detect stream completion may hang, fail to finalize the response, or silently drop content.
- **What changed:** Added `writeStopChunk()` that emits an empty-delta chunk with `finish_reason: "stop"` before every `writeDone()`. A `sentFinishReason` flag prevents double-emitting in the error path (which already wrote `finishReason: "stop"` inline). Also ensured the error catch path writes a role chunk before the error content chunk.
- **What did NOT change:** Non-streaming (sync) responses are unaffected. The error-path behavior is preserved — it still writes the error content with `finishReason: "stop"` inline; the new stop chunk is only added in the success paths.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** The lifecycle `"end"`/`"error"` handler and the `finally` fallback both wrote `writeDone(res)` + `res.end()` without a preceding stop chunk. Only the `catch` block (error path) wrote a content chunk with `finishReason: "stop"`.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** The existing streaming test (`openai-http.test.ts`) only asserts `finish_reason === "stop"` for the error case (line 845), not for normal completions.
- **Contributing context:** The non-streaming

items.title: feat: Migrate container config to API-based flow
items.content:
## Summary
Migrates container configuration from direct DynamoDB access to API-based flow through the orchestrator service. This aligns with the openclaw-agent's documented architecture requirements.

## Changes

### Architecture Migration
**Before:** Container → DynamoDB (direct access with AWS credentials)  
**After:** Container → Orchestrator Config API → DynamoDB

### Container Environment Variables
- ✅ Added: `API_KEY` - User's auth-gateway API key for config API authentication
- ✅ Added: `ORCHESTRATOR_URL` - Orchestrator API Gateway URL  
- ❌ Removed: `USER_ID` - No longer needed with API-based auth
- ❌ Removed: `DYNAMODB_TABLE` - Container doesn't access DynamoDB directly
- ❌ Removed: `DYNAMODB_REGION` - Container doesn't access DynamoDB directly

### Test Infrastructure Improvements
- Test script now automatically creates user config in DynamoDB (one-stop shop testing)
- Added container log fetching (90 seconds of CloudWatch logs)
- Fixed docker-compose filename references in local test script

### Files Changed
- `app/services/ecs.py` - Updated ECS task creation to use config API flow
- `scripts/test_end_to_end_flow.py` - Added config creation and log fetching
- `test/test_e2e.sh` - Fixed docker-compose filename (space → hyphen)

## How to Test

Run the end-to-end test script:
```bash
cd scripts
python test_end_to_end_flow.py
```

The script will:
1. Create a container via orchestrator API
2. Automatically create user config in DynamoDB
3. Wait for container to reach RUNNING status
4. Fetch and display 90 seconds of container logs
5. Verify container successfully loaded config from API

## Testing Checklist

Before merge, verify:
- [ ] `make lint` - passes
- [ ] `make test` - all unit tests pass
- [ ] `make test-integration` - integration tests pass
- [ ] End-to-end test script runs successfully
- [ ] Container logs show successful config fetch from API
- [ ] No test coverage decrease

## References
- openclaw-agent README: Documents required env vars (API_
items.author: sinkers

items.title: Memory browser: interactive UI for browsing and exploring stored memories
items.content:
## Idea

Add an interactive way to browse, search, and explore all stored memories — categories, scopes, timelines, similarity clusters.

Currently the only way to inspect memories is via CLI (`openclaw memex list`, `openclaw memex search`) which is text-only and limited. A visual browser would help with:

- Understanding what memex has stored
- Finding and cleaning up noise/duplicates
- Debugging recall issues (why didn't X surface?)
- Exploring memory clusters and relationships

## Possible approaches

- **HTML playground** — single-file self-contained explorer served via `/__memex/browser`, uses existing gateway methods (`memex.health`, SQLite queries)
- **CLI TUI** — interactive terminal UI with filtering, sorting, pagination
- **Both** — CLI for quick inspection, HTML for deep exploration

## Low priority

Nice-to-have for debugging and trust-building. Not blocking any workflow.

items.title: chore(ci): [INF-795] align CD workflows with aramiworks/ui patterns
items.content:
## Summary Aligns the CD workflows with aramiworks/ui patterns to create a full release automation chain: a changeset merge triggers a GitHub release, which automatically kicks off EAS builds and OTA updates routed to the correct environment based on the version tag. Closes INF-795. ## Type of change chore ## Key changes - **`release.yml`**: Add OpenClaw bot app token (so version PR triggers CI), `workflow_dispatch` trigger, and a `concurrency` group to prevent duplicate runs - **`eas-build.yml`**: Add `release: published` trigger with a new `resolve-params` job that parses version tags (`-dev.*` → `develop`, `-rc.*` → `stage`, stable → `master`); gates on `@stock-tracker/mobile@` prefix to avoid duplicate runs from other changesets - **`eas-update.yml`**: Add `release: published` trigger; skip `chore: version packages` commits on push; resolve channel from version tag on release; make `APP_ENV` dynamic to match the resolved channel; include release name in OTA update message ## Testing done - [ ] Manually trigger `release.yml` via `workflow_dispatch` — confirm app token generation works - [ ] Manually trigger `eas-build.yml` with `develop` profile — confirm `resolve-params` passes through inputs correctly - [ ] Manually trigger `eas-update.yml` with `develop` channel — confirm channel resolution works - [ ] End-to-end: enter dev prerelease, merge a changeset, verify release → EAS build → OTA update chain fires in sequence 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Greptile Summary

This PR wires up a full release automation chain across three CD workflows: `release.yml` gains an app token (so changeset-created version PRs can trigger CI), `eas-build.yml` adds a `release: published` trigger with tag-based profile resolution, and `eas-update.yml` adds a matching `release` trigger with channel resolution and skips the "chore: version packages" push. items.title: feat: absorb awesome-openclaw-skills and mempalace into Iris
items.content:
Absorbs the 14 Burgess-enhanced skills from [awesome-openclaw-skills](https://github.com/ljbudgie/awesome-openclaw-skills) and key concepts from [mempalace](https://github.com/ljbudgie/mempalace) into Iris's letter generation and system prompts.

### New template types (`generate-burgess-letter.ts`)

6 new types added to `templateTypes`, bringing the total from 12 → 18:

- `contract_review` — clause-by-clause contract analysis with risk flagging
- `coding_agent_review` — human-review gate for AI coding agent outputs across 7 impact areas
- `direct_debit` — bank refund disputes under the Direct Debit Guarantee
- `medical_device` — clinician review requests for algorithmic device decisions
- `music_copyright` — Content ID / royalty disputes (more specific than generic `copyright`)
- `reasonable_adjustments` — guided disability adjustment requests covering 31+ categories

### Specialized per-template prompts

Added `templateSpecificPrompts` map with detailed step-by-step workflows for all 18 templates, absorbed from the SKILL.md files. Each prompt provides domain-specific guidance (e.g., the `medical_device` prompt knows to ask for device model/serial, clinical context, and comorbidities). Injected into the LLM prompt via:

```ts
const specializedPrompt = templateSpecificPrompts[templateType];
// ...appended to the generation prompt when present
```

### System prompt enrichment (`prompts.ts`)

- **Template table** updated with the 6 new types
- **Human-impact scanner** — 7 Burgess impact areas (accessibility, privacy, security, user language, billing, automated decisions, deployment) from `mempalace/burgess.py`. Iris will flag these in conversation when code/deployment topics arise.
- **MemPalace architecture awareness** — palace structure (wings → rooms → closets → drawers), 4-layer memory stack, AAAK dialect. Enables Iris to guide users on persistent AI memory.

items.author: bisdom-cell

items.title: feat(governance): 3 runtime invariants — from declaration to runtime …
items.content:
…truth

Today's real bugs drove 3 new invariants (12→15 total):

- INV-CRON-003: bash-lc-enforcement — every enabled job's crontab entry must include `bash -lc` (otherwise env vars are missing in cron)
- INV-CRON-004: crontab-no-duplicates — no duplicate entries for the same job (causes concurrent execution and lock contention)
- INV-ENV-002: cron-env-smoke-test — OPENCLAW_PHONE must be real phone number in cron env, not placeholder +85200000000

Dimension leap: from "declaration consistency" to "runtime truth". Lesson: 4-layer checks all passed while WhatsApp push silently failed for 3 days because they only asked "what's declared" never "what actually happens at runtime".

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CHUYbEh7oe612yVWES1LwX

items.title: Recurring isolated cron jobs have unstable first-turn OpenAI prompt-cache hits across runs
items.content:
## Summary

Prompt caching on `openai/*` / `openai-codex/*` appears to work well *within* a single run, but recurring `sessionTarget: "isolated"` cron jobs still show unstable first-turn cache hits across runs, even when the job prompt and static file inputs are effectively unchanged and `cacheRetention: "long"` is enabled.

In practice this makes recurring cron workflows much less cache-efficient than expected, especially for jobs that run every 30 minutes and repeatedly send the same large prompt prefix.

## What I observed

On a local OpenClaw deployment:

- recurring cron job
- `sessionTarget: "isolated"`
- model: `openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini`
- `agents.defaults.params.cacheRetention = "long"`
- `lightContext: true`
- same cron job prompt each run
- same static strategy file read each run

Across the last 28 `gpt-5.4-mini` runs of the same cron job, the **first assistant request** had nonzero `cacheRead` only **4/28 times (~14%)**.

However, within the *same* run, later turns often do hit cache strongly.

That pattern suggests:
- prompt caching is enabled
- the repeated prefix is large enough to benefit
- but cross-run routing affinity for isolated cron runs is not stable enough for reliable first-turn cache reuse

## Concrete evidence

For two recent back-to-back runs of the same recurring isolated cron job, the early turn usage looked like this:

Run A:
- turn 1: `input=15959`, `cacheRead=0`
- turn 2: `input=40688`, `cacheRead=15872`
- turn 3: `input=979`, `cacheRead=56320`

Run B:
- turn 1: `input=15959`, `cacheRead=0`
- turn 2: `input=43952`, `cacheRead=15872`
- turn 3: `input=4470`, `cacheRead=59392`

So even when the runs are consecutive and the job prompt is effectively unchanged:
- first turn still misses
- second turn begins to partially hit
- later turns hit strongly

This is consistent with OpenAI cache reuse *within* a run, but weak cross-run affinity for isolated cron jobs.

## Why this matters

For recurring cron workloads, the first turn is often th
items.author: lubao515

items.title: FEATURE: Add a same-session corpus ingest primitive for text files
items.content:
### Summary

Add a first-class way to ingest multiple text files into the active session context with deterministic full-file traversal, clear completion guarantees, and source anchors. This should support workflows where the agent must read a defined prerequisite corpus before drafting or making decisions.

### Problem to solve

The current plain-text path relies on repeated read-tool calls, which are optimized for inspection rather than one-shot corpus ingestion. In large multi-file workflows, that creates friction around truncation, pagination, context residency, and proof of completion. Users who need the current session to ingest a prerequisite corpus before writing or deciding do not have a reliable first-class primitive for that job.

### Proposed solution

Introduce a same-session text-corpus ingest primitive, such as a new files_ingest tool or a session preload / corpus attach capability. The primitive should: (1) accept an ordered list of text files, (2) traverse every file to EOF internally, (3) load the corpus into the active session context rather than a detached worker, (4) return a deterministic manifest with file paths, line counts, bytes, checksums or equivalent integrity markers, and success/failure status per file, (5) preserve source anchors such as file and line ranges when possible, and (6) fail honestly if the requested corpus cannot be fully resident in the current session. The key distinction is that this should be an ingestion primitive, not just an inspection tool with manual pagination.

### Alternatives considered

Continue using repeated read-tool pagination, rely on external preprocessing or background workers, or use document-specific ingest paths such as PDF handling. These can help in md some cases, but they do not cleanly satisfy workflows that require the current session to personally ingest a multi-file text corpus before the next stage.

### Impact

This would improve reliability for source-heavy workflows such as prerequisite r

This fixes the repo-loss issue we saw on Pluto after container recreation.

What changed:

items.title: RFC: Mock services infrastructure for API-dependent tasks
items.content:
## Problem

Many real-world OpenClaw tasks involve external APIs that we can't reliably test against:
- **Rate limits** — Real APIs throttle, making benchmarks inconsistent
- **Cost** — Some APIs charge per call
- **State mutation** — Real CRMs/calendars would get polluted
- **Reproducibility** — External data changes over time
- **Grading** — Hard to verify correct API usage without seeing what was called

We need mock services that behave like real APIs but are controlled, observable, and free.

## Proposed Mock Services

### Critical (High-value OpenClaw use cases)

| Service | Why | Example Tasks |
|---------|-----|---------------|
| **Calendar API** | Scheduling is core OpenClaw UX | "Schedule a meeting", "Find free slots", "Check conflicts" |

| **Weather API** | Common info lookup | "What's the weather", "Should I bring umbrella" |

items.author: rocdesu

items.title: Claude PR review workflow can approve despite 🟡 IMPORTANT findings
items.content:
## Summary
The GitHub Actions workflow `.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml` says Claude should submit a `REQUEST_CHANGES` review if there are any 🔴 CRITICAL or 🟡 IMPORTANT findings. However, on PR #1008 Claude labeled an issue as **🟡 IMPORTANT** and still submitted an **APPROVED** review.

This means the current workflow is too lenient in practice because severity-to-review-state is enforced only by prompt instructions, not by executable logic.

## Evidence
Workflow policy in `main`:
- If there are any 🔴 CRITICAL or 🟡 IMPORTANT issues → `gh pr review --request-changes`
- If there are only 🔵 SUGGESTIONs or no issues → `gh pr review --approve`

Observed on PR #1008:
- Claude comment included:
  - `🟡 IMPORTANT — MSI-High node: GO:0002376 is the broadest possible immune process term`
- Claude review body also said:
  - `One 🟡 IMPORTANT item: GO:0002376 ... is too broad ... should be substituted.`
- But GitHub review state was still:
  - `APPROVED`

## Root cause
The workflow delegates the severity -> review action decision entirely to Claude via prompt text. There is no hard validation layer that checks the generated review summary or structured result before allowing an approval review.

So the model can drift into behavior like:
- mark something as IMPORTANT in prose
- then still say “ready to merge”
- and submit `--approve`

## Why this matters
This undermines the intended review gate. A reviewer can surface biologically or ontologically important issues while still granting approval, which defeats the purpose of using severity labels in the first place.

## Suggested fix
Add a hard enforcement step after Claude produces its output. Some options:

1. **Structured-output approach (preferred)**
   - Make Claude emit machine-readable JSON with:
     - `review_state: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES`
     - `counts: {critical, important, suggestion}`
   - Fail the workflow if `important > 0 || critical > 0` and `review_state == APPROVE`

2. **Post-hoc text validation**

items.title: [Bug]:Isolated cron sessions fail OpenRouter auth with 401 User not found
items.content:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

# Isolated cron jobs cannot access OpenRouter credentials despite main session working

Isolated cron jobs (`sessionTarget: "isolated"`) fail with HTTP 401 from OpenRouter immediately on launch. Main sessions with the same credentials work perfectly.

## Environment

- OpenClaw: 2026.3.30 (confirmed persists on 2026.4.6)
- Platform: Docker on Ubuntu (local, Docker Compose)
- Provider: OpenRouter (via `openclaw models auth add`)
- Session target: `sessionTarget: "isolated"`

## Expected Behavior

Isolated cron sessions should resolve provider credentials the same way main sessions do, via any of:
- `auth-profiles.json` (populated by `openclaw models auth add`)
- `openclaw.json` env block
- Process environment variables

## Current Behavior

Isolated cron jobs fail within ~2 seconds with `HTTP 401: User not found` from OpenRouter — before agent execution begins. Main interactive sessions work perfectly. `openclaw models status --probe` confirms auth is valid.

The same cron job with `model: "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"` succeeds every time.

## Minimal Reproduction

1. Add OpenRouter auth:
```bash
openclaw models auth add
# Select OpenRouter, enter a valid API key
openclaw models status --probe  # ✅ OpenRouter shows ok

2. Create a cron job targeting an OpenRouter model:
openclaw cron add \
  --name "test" \
  --cron "0 13 * * *" \
  --session isolated \
  --model "openrouter/google/gemini-2.5-flash" \
  --message "Say hello."

3. Trigger immediately:
openclaw cron run <job-id>
Result: HTTP 401: User not found from OpenRouter within ~2 seconds.
Same job with anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 works correctly.

Debug Info

Main session confirms credentials are valid:
# Direct API call succeeds
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $OPENROUTER_API_KEY" \
  https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models
# HTTP 200 ✅

# OpenClaw probe succeeds
openclaw models status --probe
# openrouter:

items.title: fix: resolve 6 issues found during 0.2.0-beta.1 smoke testing
items.content:
## Summary

End-to-end smoke testing of the `0.2.0-beta.1` npm release on both Base and Taiko uncovered 6 issues. This PR fixes all of them.

### Issues Fixed

- **`@trustedagents/sdk` never published to npm** — The SDK was missing from `bump-version.sh`, `release.yml`, and `verify-release-packages.mjs`. Added it to all three, bumped version to `0.2.0-beta.1`, and added `files` field + README for tarball correctness.
- **OWS API keys revoked by test cleanup** — `ows-cleanup.ts` scanned all `tap-` prefixed keys in the shared vault and revoked them, destroying real agent keys when tests ran on the same machine. Now snapshots pre-existing key IDs in `beforeEach` and only revokes keys created during the test.
- **`tap remove` blocked by `outbox/` directory** — TAP creates `outbox/` (with `queued/`, `processing/`, `results/` subdirs) during normal operation but it wasn't in the removal whitelist. Added it.
- **Inconsistent OWS policy chain scope** — `tap init --chain taiko` unconditionally injected Base into the policy chain list, while Base-only init didn't add Taiko. Removed the asymmetric injection.
- **Raw viem errors leaked to users** — Servo RPC failures surfaced full viem stack traces (contract addresses, hex data, version). Wrapped with `ConnectionError` and added `shortMessage` preference to `toErrorMessage` as a safety net for all viem errors.
- **Unhelpful IPFS timeout error** — `identity resolve-self` after `register update` showed a generic abort error. Now distinguishes timeout from permanent failure with a retry hint.

## Test plan

- [x] All 669 unit/integration tests pass
- [x] Build succeeds for all packages
- [ ] Re-run `npm install -g trusted-agents-cli@<next-beta>` after publishing with SDK included
- [ ] Verify `tap remove --unsafe-wipe-data-dir --yes` works when `outbox/` exists
- [ ] Verify `tap init --chain taiko` policy only covers Taiko

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

items.title: Cron tasks in-flight during gateway restart are lost
items.content:
## Problem

When the OpenClaw gateway restarts (e.g. during updates or debugging), any cron tasks currently in-flight are orphaned and marked as `lost`. There's no mechanism to:

1. **Detect** that a task was running when the gateway went down
2. **Resume** or **retry** the task after the gateway comes back up
3. **Alert** that tasks were lost due to a restart (vs a genuine failure)

## Impact

For users who restart the gateway frequently (debugging, config changes, updates), this means cron tasks silently fail on a regular basis. The only way to discover this is manually running `openclaw tasks list --status=lost`.

Tasks affected include anything that happens to be running at restart time — health checks, automation scripts, scheduled digests, etc.

## Proposed Solutions (any would help)

- **Retry on restart:** When the gateway starts, check for tasks that were `running` at shutdown time and re-queue them
- **Graceful shutdown:** Wait for in-flight tasks to complete before shutting down (with a timeout)
- **Lost task webhook/notification:** Fire an event when tasks are marked as lost so a watchdog can pick it up
- **Status distinction:** Differentiate `lost_restart` from `lost_crash` so users know the cause

## Context

Reported by a user running ~12 cron jobs across multiple agents. Gateway restarts during debugging cause 2-5 lost tasks per incident. The workaround is a watchdog cron that polls `openclaw tasks list` for `lost`/`timed_out` status, but this is reactive and misses the root cause.

items.title: [Bug]: Chrome MCP attach fails when OpenClaw passes both --browserUrl and --userDataDir (mutually exclusive), plus intermittent browser status flakiness
items.content:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

OpenClaw fails Chrome MCP attach when configured with explicit browserUrl/wsEndpoint because it also passes userDataDir. chrome-devtools-mcp treats those as mutually exclusive, then connection closes. Also, browser status is still intermittent even after practical actions recovered.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure OpenClaw Chrome attach with explicit endpoint (browserUrl to local CDP, e.g. http://127.0.0.1:9222)
2. Run browser attach through OpenClaw MCP integration
3. Observe OpenClaw passes both endpoint and userDataDir
4. MCP returns mutual-exclusion error and closes connection




### Expected behavior

When browserUrl or wsEndpoint is set, OpenClaw should not pass userDataDir.


### Actual behavior

Arguments userDataDir and browserUrl are mutually exclusive
Then: MCP error -32000: Connection closed
Also observed: browser status remains intermittent while open/tabs/snapshot/act work.

### OpenClaw version

2026.4.6

### Operating system

items.title: feat(release): add prerelease support
items.content:
Prerelease versions can already be tagged in this repo, but the release workflow and installer still treated every build as stable. This routes prerelease tags to npm `beta`, adds `--channel` and `--version` support to both `scripts/install.sh` and `tap install`, and keeps OpenClaw plugin installs aligned with the selected CLI release.

The README, release docs, and TAP skill now document the stable-vs-prerelease paths. Focused prerelease tests, lint, and workspace typecheck all pass.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Medium risk because it changes the release/publish workflow and installation paths (npm dist-tags, GitHub prerelease flag, OpenClaw plugin install spec), which can affect how users receive updates if misconfigured.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds prerelease support across release, install, and docs.
> 
> The release workflow now detects prerelease tag versions via `scripts/release-channel.mjs`, publishes packages with an npm dist-tag (e.g. `beta`) when appropriate, and marks GitHub Releases as *prereleases*.
> 
> The installer and CLI `tap install` now accept `--channel` and `--version` selectors, propagating them through to ensure OpenClaw plugin installs use the matching package spec, with new tests covering selector behavior. Docs (`README.md`, `docs/release.md`, skill) were updated to document stable vs prerelease usage, and old superpowers plan/spec docs were removed.
> 
> <sup>Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit 9dc6f457afaed97f97e8271561988f4f685797f4. Bugbot is set up for automated code reviews on this repo. Configure [here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: `message send` crashes with `ERR_INTERNAL_ASSERTION` in v2026.4.5 (regression from v2026.4.2)
items.content:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

On v2026.4.5, `openclaw message send` exits immediately with `Error [ERR_INTERNAL_ASSERTION]` thrown from `command-registry-CNkzrn92.js`, while the same command on v2026.4.2 (same image, same config, same host) succeeds and returns a Message ID.

### Steps to reproduce

```bash
cd ~/projects/openclaw
git fetch --tags
git checkout v2026.4.5
docker build -t openclaw:local -f Dockerfile .
docker compose up -d

# Sanity checks — all OK on 2026.4.5
docker exec openclaw-openclaw-gateway-1 node dist/index.js --version
# -> 2026.4.5  ✅
docker exec openclaw-openclaw-gateway-1 node dist/index.js doctor
# -> all green ✅
docker exec openclaw-openclaw-gateway-1 node dist/index.js channels status
# -> whatsapp connected ✅

# The failing command
docker exec openclaw-openclaw-gateway-1 node dist/index.js message send \
  --channel whatsapp --target '+33XXXXXXXXX' --message 'test'
# -> CRASH (stack trace below)
```

### Expected behavior

`message send --channel whatsapp --target '+33XXXXXXXXX' --message '...'` should dispatch the message via the WhatsApp channel and return a Message ID, as it does on v2026.4.2 (latency ~26s, dominated by Baileys handshake — measured and confirmed normal).

### Actual behavior

The command crashes immediately with a Node.js internal assertion **before** any send attempt is made. The crash occurs at module-registration time:

```
Error [ERR_INTERNAL_ASSERTION]:

This is caused by either a bug in Node.js or incorrect usage of Node.js internals.
Please open an issue with this stack trace at https://github.com/nodejs/node/issues

  at loadCJSModuleWithModuleLoad (node:internal/modules/esm/translators:325:3)
  at async Object.register (file:///app/dist/command-registry-CNkzrn92.js:95:5)
```

No graceful error message. No indication of which module failed to load. Process exits non-zero with no telemetry.

### OpenClaw version

v2026.4.5 (commit d9e4d607) — las

items.author: ThomasLiu

items.title: Bootstrap DrClaw: Edge-safe UUID, Paperclip agent init, Student Simulator + Next.js 15.3.9 security upgrade
items.content:
Initializes DrClaw from an empty repo. Addresses the Vercel `crypto.randomUUID()` Edge Runtime failure, wires up Paperclip agent orchestration, builds the Student Simulator MVP, and patches multiple Next.js DoS/RCE CVEs.

## Infrastructure: Edge-compatible UUID

`crypto.randomUUID()` is not guaranteed in Vercel's Edge Runtime. Replaced all direct usage with a shared utility:

```ts
// lib/uuid.ts — used everywhere (middleware, auth, API routes)
export function generateId(): string {
  if (typeof crypto?.randomUUID === 'function') return crypto.randomUUID()
  if (typeof crypto?.getRandomValues === 'function') return uuidV4ViaGetRandomValues()
  throw new Error('generateId() requires the Web Crypto API')  // no Math.random() fallback
}
```

`Math.random()` fallback intentionally omitted — IDs are used in session tokens.

## Paperclip Agent Orchestration

- **`company.json`** — defines four agents (CEO, UX Lead, Eng Lead, Content Lead) with model assignments, Redis state keys, and heartbeat intervals
- **`CLAUDE.md`** — master instructions file covering Edge Runtime rules, API route conventions, and per-agent responsibilities
- **`/api/agents/heartbeat`** (GET + POST) — agents register presence in Upstash Redis with 5-min TTL auto-expiry; GET returns live status of all agents
- **`agents/`** — per-agent markdown instruction files

## Student Simulator

`POST /api/simulate-student` (Content Lead / `claude-haiku-4-5`): accepts a Markdown assignment brief and a `studentProfile` (`junior` | `mid` | `senior`) and returns a simulated student submission. Full UI at `/simulator`.

## Next.js CVE patch: 14.2.29 → 15.3.9

`next@14.2.29` is affected by three vulnerability families (DoS via Server Components deserialization, incomplete fix follow-up, HTTP request deserialization DoS). The `>= 13.0.0, < 15.0.8` range is only patchable by moving to 15.x; `15.3.9` is confirmed clean in the GitHub Advisory DB.

Requires one config migration:
```js
// next.config.js — Next.js 15 rename

items.title: fix(mcp): MCP server crashes with Cannot find module 'bun:sqlite' under Node
items.content:
## Summary

- v12.0.0 ships an MCP server bundle that crashes on the very first `require()` because it tries to load `bun:sqlite` while running under Node — completely breaking the MCP tool surface for Codex/MCP-only clients and any flow that boots the MCP server.
- Root cause: commit 2b60dd29 added `import { ensureWorkerStarted } from '../services/worker-service.js'` to `src/servers/mcp-server.ts`. That import transitively pulls in the entire worker service (DatabaseManager, ChromaSync, etc.), which in turn imports `bun:sqlite`. The bundle ballooned from ~358KB (v11.0.1) to ~1.96MB (v12.0.0) and `node mcp-server.cjs` immediately throws ``Error: Cannot find module 'bun:sqlite'``.
- Fix: extract the daemon-spawn helpers into a new lightweight `src/services/worker-spawner.ts` (no SQLite imports), wire `mcp-server.ts` and `worker-service.ts` through that module, and make `resolveWorkerRuntimePath` / `spawnDaemon` find Bun on every platform — not just Windows — so the MCP server (running under Node) can correctly spawn the worker daemon (which must run under Bun).

## What changed

- **New** `src/services/worker-spawner.ts` — canonical `ensureWorkerStarted(port, workerScriptPath)` plus the Windows spawn-cooldown helpers, importing only `infrastructure/ProcessManager`, `infrastructure/HealthMonitor`, `shared/*`, and `utils/logger`. Zero database deps.
- **`src/services/worker-service.ts`** — drops the in-file copies of `ensureWorkerStarted` / `shouldSkipSpawnOnWindows` / `markWorkerSpawnAttempted` / `clearWorkerSpawnAttempted` / `getWorkerSpawnLockPath`. Keeps a one-line back-compat wrapper that passes `__filename` so existing CLI/hook callers behave identically.
- **`src/servers/mcp-server.ts`** — imports `ensureWorkerStarted` from `worker-spawner.js` and resolves `WORKER_SCRIPT_PATH` next to the MCP bundle so it can ask the spawner to start `worker-service.cjs` without ever importing it.
- **`src/services/infrastructure/ProcessManager.ts`** — `resolveWorkerRuntimePath(

## Tokimo 是什么

一句话：**一个运行在浏览器里的 AI 原生云桌面操作系统。**

但「一句话」掩盖了它真正的野心。

Tokimo 不是一个 dashboard，不是一个 NAS 管理面板，不是一个媒体服务器的替代品。它试图做的事情是：**把你所有的个人数据、工作流、娱乐消费，统一到一个具备完整桌面窗口体验的系统里，让 AI 在这个系统里直接操作、直接展示结果**。

举几个真实的场景：

你昨天在照片 App 里翻了儿子满月的照片；今天早上手机又同步了一批新的；下午开完会，会议纪要落在知识库里。你什么都没说，Tokimo 已经知道

items.title: fix: stale tool name reference and inconsistent msg_type enum in send_message_to_agent
items.content:
## Pre-checks

- [x] I have searched [existing issues](https://github.com/dataelement/Clawith/issues) and this is not a duplicate.

## Deployment Method

Source (setup.sh)

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create two native agents A and B with an `AgentAgentRelationship` between them
2. Agent A's `relationships.md` is generated with the hint: `可以用 send_agent_message 工具给 B 发消息协作` (You can use the send_agent_message tool to send messages to B for collaboration)
3. Agent A attempts to call `send_agent_message` to send a message to B
4. The tool `send_agent_message` does not exist in the tool registry — only `send_message_to_agent` is registered, causing the call to fail

## Expected vs Actual Behavior

**Expected**: Agents are guided to call the correct tool name `send_message_to_agent` based on prompts in `relationships.md` and gateway communication alerts.

**Actual**: Two prompt locations reference the non-existent tool name `send_agent_message`, which misleads the LLM and causes tool call failures or unexpected behavior.

## Details

### Issue 1: Stale tool name references (2 locations)

| File | Line | Current (incorrect) | Should be |
|------|------|---------------------|-----------|
| `backend/app/api/relationships.py` | L306 | `send_agent_message` | `send_message_to_agent` |
| `backend/app/api/gateway.py` | L416 | `` `send_agent_message` `` | `` `send_message_to_agent` `` |

**Impact analysis**:

- **`relationships.py:306`**: This text is written into the agent's `relationships.md` workspace file, which is injected into the LLM context. The LLM is guided to call `send_agent_message`, which does not exist in the tool registry. This increases the probability of tool call failures.
- **`gateway.py:416`**: This prompt is injected into OpenClaw agents receiving an inter-agent message, telling them *"DO NOT use the send_agent_message tool to reply"*. Since `send_agent_message` does not exist, the LLM may instead attempt to call the similarly-named `send_message_to_agent` (w

items.title: [Bug]: Models generating text-based tool calls send raw XML to messaging channels
items.content:
## Bug Description

When models generate tool calls as TEXT (XML format like `<minimax:tool_call><invoke name="exec">...` or MiniMax's `<invoke>...</invoke>`) instead of proper structured tool calls (`tool_calls[]`), the raw XML syntax is sent directly to users on messaging channels like WhatsApp.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Use a model via modelrelay (or any provider)
2. Ask the model to perform a task that would trigger tool usage
3. If the model generates tool calls as text instead of structured tool_calls
4. The raw XML tool call syntax appears in the WhatsApp/message response

## Expected Behavior

- Models should use structured `tool_calls[]` format
- If text tool calls appear, they should be stripped from user-facing output
- Users should see either:
  - A clean conversational response (if no tool execution needed)
  - Tool execution results (if structured tool calls were used)

## Actual Behavior

Users see raw XML like:
```
Let me check what reminders are active:
<minimax:tool_call>
<invoke name=\"exec\">...
```

This happens because:
1. The model's response has `stopReason: "stop"` (not tool_calls)
2. Tool call XML is in `content[].text` not `tool_calls[]`
3. `sanitizeUserFacingText()` only strips `<final>` tags, not tool call XML
4. The text passes through and is delivered to the messaging channel

## Root Cause

In `src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/errors.ts`, the `stripFinalTagsFromText()` function only strips `<final>` tags:

```typescript
function stripFinalTagsFromText(text: unknown): string {
  const normalized = coerceText(text);
  if (!normalized) {
    return normalized;
  }
  return normalized.replace(FINAL_TAG_RE, "");  // Only strips <final> tags
}
```

The function `stripToolCallXmlTags()` exists in `src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.ts` and handles `<tool_call>`, `<invoke>`, etc., but it's not called in the message delivery path.

## Suggested Fix

Call `stripToolCallXmlTags()` in `stripFinalTagsFromText()`:

```typescript
import { stripTo

items.author: CryogenicPlanet

items.title: sessions_send A2A flow ping-pong causes duplicate messages in persistent sessions
items.content:
## Summary

When using `sessions_send` between two persistent agent sessions, the A2A flow triggers a **ping-pong reply phase** (`DEFAULT_PING_PONG_TURNS = 5`) that injects messages into both sessions via the `agent` API. These injected messages persist in the target session and get processed as new user messages after the current agent run completes, causing duplicate message delivery.

## Reproduction

1. Agent A calls `sessions_send` to Agent B (both persistent sessions, e.g. `agent:main:main` → `agent:lead:feishu:group:...`)
2. Agent B replies → reply returned inline to Agent A ✅
3. A2A flow automatically triggers ping-pong phase
4. Ping-pong alternates `runAgentStep()` calls, injecting messages into both Agent A and Agent B sessions
5. Agent A processes the injected messages as new user messages → duplicates ❌

## Root Cause

In `src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.ts`, `runSessionsSendA2AFlow`:

1. After the initial reply, it resolves an `announceTarget` and runs **ping-pong turns** (default 5, max 5)
2. Each ping-pong turn calls `runAgentStep()` which uses `callGateway({ method: "agent", sessionKey, inputProvenance: { kind: "inter_session", sourceSessionKey, sourceTool: "sessions_send" } })`
3. These `agent` API calls persist messages directly into the target session (the `deliver: false` flag only prevents external channel delivery, not session persistence)
4. When the requesting agent's run completes, the injected messages are queued and processed as new user messages

The ping-pong mechanism is designed for `sessions_spawn` (sub-agent) scenarios where a brief back-and-forth makes sense. For persistent session-to-session communication where the reply is already returned inline, it creates unnecessary duplicates.

## Expected Behavior

`sessions_send` between persistent sessions should only return the reply inline, without injecting additional messages into either session via the ping-pong phase.

## Suggested Fix

1. Add a `maxPingPongTurns` parameter t
items.author: tonyhth

items.title: npm CLI rejects valid streaming config from source build
items.content:
## Problem

The npm-published CLI (`@anthropic/openclaw`) rejects `streaming` config values that the source build accepts. Running any CLI command (e.g. `openclaw plugins list`) with a config from a recent source build produces:

```
Invalid config at ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json:
- channels.discord.streaming: Invalid option: expected one of "off"|"partial"|"block"|"progress"
- channels.discord.accounts.kagura.streaming: Invalid option: expected one of "off"|"partial"|"block"|"progress"
```

## Context

The source build (main branch) now supports a richer `streaming` config object (beyond the simple enum), but the npm release still validates against the old enum-only schema.

This means:
- Users who run from source cannot use `openclaw` CLI commands from the npm package
- Third-party tools (e.g. `pew`) that call `openclaw plugins` also break
- There's no graceful degradation — the CLI refuses to start entirely

## Expected behavior

Either:
1. The npm release should accept the same streaming config as source, or
2. The CLI should warn about unknown streaming values but not refuse to start (forward-compatible validation)

## Environment

- Source build: main branch (latest as of 2026-04-08)
- npm CLI: latest published version
- Config generated by source build gateway

items.title: fix(msteams): send pairing challenge reply on unpaired DMs
items.content:
## Summary

- Problem: With `channels.msteams.dmPolicy=pairing`, unpaired users had pairing requests recorded on disk but received no Teams message with the pair code, making first-time setup depend on host access to `msteams-pairing.json` or CLI listing.
- Why it matters: Operators could see HTTP 200 on the Bot Framework webhook and no agent reply, with little in-channel or structured feedback to distinguish pairing from connectivity or auth failures.
- What changed: The MS Teams monitor now uses the shared `issuePairingChallenge` path (`pairing.issueChallenge`): on the first pairing upsert for a sender it sends the standard pairing text via `context.sendActivity`, and logs `msteams pairing request` with `sender`, `code`, and `label` at info; reply failures are logged at error.
- What did NOT change: Group/channel routing, allowlists, HTTP adapter behavior, or pairing store format; repeat messages from the same unpaired sender still do not re-send the challenge (same as other channels using `issuePairingChallenge`).

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

- [x] Integrations

- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #62765
- Related #

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The MS Teams DM gate called `upsertPairingRequest` directly and never invoked the shared pairing challenge flow that sends the in-channel reply on newly created requests.
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A (behavior was intentional omission; parity with Signal/Mattermost-style pairing replies was missing).

items.title: fix(tools): expand tilde in host edit/write paths (non-workspace mode)
items.content:
## Summary

- **Problem:** The edit and write tools fail when given tilde paths (e.g. `~/.openclaw/workspace/memory/2026-04-07.md`) in non-workspace mode. `path.resolve("~/foo")` treats `~` as a literal directory name, resolving to `<cwd>/~/foo` instead of expanding to the user's home directory.
- **Why it matters:** Users on Telegram (and other channels) see `⚠️ 📝 Edit: in ~/.openclaw/workspace/memory/... failed` errors when the agent tries to edit files via tilde paths. Reported as a regression in #62517.
- **What changed:** Added `expandHomePrefix()` (already used in 13 other files) before `path.resolve()` in the 5 non-workspace host edit/write call sites in `pi-tools.read.ts`.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** The workspace-only (`workspaceOnly: true`) code path already expands tilde correctly via `readFileWithinRoot`/`writeFileWithinRoot` in `fs-safe.ts`. That path is untouched. Sandbox edit operations are also untouched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #62517
- Related #30669
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** `createHostEditOperations` and `createHostWriteOperations` in `src/agents/pi-tools.read.ts` use bare `path.resolve(absolutePath)` in the non-workspace branch. Node's `path.resolve` treats `~` as a literal directory character, not a home directory alias. The workspace-only branch already calls `expandHomePrefix()` via `fs-safe.ts`, but the non-workspace branch was missed.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No test covered tilde-prefixed paths through the non-workspace host edit/write operations.
- **Contributing context:** The workspa
items.author: stainlu

items.title: CLI hangs indefinitely on 'openclaw memory index --force' and 'openclaw cron list' after gateway restart (v2026.4.5)
items.content:
## Bug Report

### Version
OpenClaw v2026.4.5 (upgraded from v2026.3.31)

### Environment
- OS: Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS
- Node.js: v22.22.2
- Architecture: x64

### Description
After upgrading to v2026.4.5 and after any `openclaw gateway restart`, certain CLI commands hang indefinitely with no output and no timeout.

### Affected Commands
- `openclaw memory index --force`
- `openclaw cron list`

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Run `openclaw update` to upgrade to v2026.4.5
2. Run `openclaw gateway restart` (or restart via systemd)
3. Run `openclaw memory index --force` or `openclaw cron list`
4. Command hangs indefinitely — no output, no error, no timeout

### Additional Observations
- On first gateway start after upgrade, 3 `openclaw-cron` worker processes spawn with 100%+ CPU usage each (zombie/stuck state)
- Manually killing these processes with `kill -9` and restarting gateway resolves the cron worker issue
- However CLI commands continue to hang even after cron workers are cleaned up
- The gateway itself runs normally (API, sessions, cron jobs all function correctly)
- Only CLI commands that communicate with the gateway are affected

### Workaround
Use OpenClaw native tools (e.g. `cron` tool with `action=list`) instead of CLI commands. Avoid `openclaw memory index` until fixed.

### Related
Possibly related to issue #19874 (similar CLI hang reported in v2026.2.17)

### Impact
P2 — gateway and core functionality unaffected, CLI tooling unusable
items.author: qingcaohuang

items.title: [Release] openclaw 2026.4.7
items.content:
### Changes
- CLI/infer: add a first-class `openclaw infer ...` hub for provider-backed inference workflows across model, media, web, and embedding tasks. Thanks @Takhoffman.
- Tools/media generation: auto-fallback across auth-backed image, music, and video providers by default, preserve intent during provider switches, remap size/aspect/resolution/duration hints to the closest supported option, and surface provider capabilities plus mode-aware video-to-video support.
- Memory/wiki: restore the bundled `memory-wiki` stack with plugin, CLI, sync/query/apply tooling, memory-host integration, structured claim/evidence fields, compiled digest retrieval, claim-health linting, contradiction clustering, staleness dashboards, and freshness-weighted search. Thanks @vincentkoc.
- Plugins/webhooks: add a bundled webhook ingress plugin so external automation can create and drive bound TaskFlows through per-route shared-secret endpoints. (#61892) Thanks @mbelinky.
- Gateway/sessions: add persisted compaction checkpoints plus Sessions UI branch/restore actions so operators can inspect and recover pre-compaction session state. (#62146) Thanks @scoootscooob.
- Compaction: add pluggable compaction provider registry so plugins can replace the built-in summarization pipeline. Configure via `agents.defaults.compaction.provider`; falls back to LLM summarization on provider failure. (#56224) Thanks @DhruvBhatia0.
- Agents/system prompt: add `agents.defaults.systemPromptOverride` for controlled prompt experiments plus heartbeat prompt-section controls so heartbeat runtime behavior can stay enabled without injecting heartbeat instructions every turn.
- Providers/Google: add Gemma 4 model support and keep Google fallback resolution on the requested provider path so native Google Gemma routes work again. (#61507) Thanks @eyjohn.
- Providers/Google: preserve explicit thinking-off semantics for Gemma 4 while still enabling Gemma reasoning support in compatibility wrappers. (#62127) Thanks @rom
